title: UFC great’s future up in the air after dominant defeat: ‘I was compromised’
description: Former welterweight king Kamaru Usman fell to a middleweight defeat by Dricus Du Plessis, who bounced back from his title loss with a one-sided points win
fullText:
Kamaru Usman’s UFC future is up in the air after he suffered a one-sided loss to Dricus Du Plessis on Saturday.

Former welterweight king Usman was competing up at middleweight as he faced Du Plessis, who earned scores of 50-45, 49-46 and 49-46 in their main-event in Oklahoma City.

It was a crucial comeback for “DDP”, whose last outing was a middleweight-title loss to Khamzat Chimaev in August, a comprehensive points defeat.

The South African, 32, clearly outstruck Usman, 39, and neutralised the veteran’s usually-considerable wrestling threat. Usman appeared to be on wobbly legs on numerous occasions, thanks to Du Plessis’s strikes, while in other moments he ambitiously looked to the referee in hopes that fouls would be called – largely to no avail.

“I couldn’t get going,” Usman said at a post-fight press conference. “He was big, he was strong, he was rangy. So, it was a little tough to get going. I took kind of a thumb-punch in the eye early on, and that kind of blurred the vision a bit. But no excuses, it is what it is.

“I was pretty compromised [by the ‘thumb-punch’]. Like I said, he’s a big boy, and when you start seeing two of the big boys, and he’s got kind of a weird, rangy shots... it’s a little delayed to get out of the way of those shots, seeing them. But no excuses.

“It’s tough to be on this side. I’m not used to being on this side, so yeah, it kind of sucks.”

The Nigerian-American also hinted at pre-fight issues, saying cryptically: “My [in]ability to do what I want kind of surprised me, because like I said: each and every fight, we’re dealing with something, but you guys never hear of it – especially with me, because most of the time I come out here and I’m victorious, so you never hear about what I’m dealing with coming in.

“But when you’re dealing with something, and it kind of hinders you and your ability to go out there and do exactly what you want to do, fight the way that you know you can fight, it kind of sucks a bit.”

It was a fourth defeat in five fights for the former pound-for-pound No 1, whose 2022 title loss to Leon Edwards gave way to a second defeat by the British-Jamaican, a narrow points defeat by Chimaev on Usman’s middleweight debut, then a clinic against Joaquin Buckley back at welterweight last June.

And while Usman refused to call time on his career on Saturday, his next move is unclear.

“We’ll see where there’s next opportunities at,” he said. “Listen, opportunities always come up, like taking on Khazmat Chimaev on eight, seven days’ notice – whatever that was. Opportunity came up. [Fighting Jorge] Masvidal on six days’ notice, opportunity came up.

“And there’s been a couple of little ones that didn’t necessarily materialise, that I’ve kind of almost jumped in line for. So, opportunities always come up, and look: Michael Bisping was middleweight champion of the world. Let’s not forget that opportunity came up, he jumped on it, he was a middleweight champion [aged 37, in his 26th UFC fight].”

Meanwhile, Du Plessis said, “I’m back in the winner’s circle, let’s get my belt back,” and his chances of doing so might have drastically improved in recent months.

After Chimaev’s dominant wrestling display against DDP in August, the Russian lost the belt in his first title defence, a shock points defeat by Sean Strickland in May. Chimaev had never been beaten before, while Strickland’s win made him a two-time middleweight champion.

And it was Du Plessis who ended the American’s first title reign in 2024, outpointing Strickland before doing the same 11 months later to retain the gold.

Du Plessis also recorded a successful title defence against Israel Adesanya between those fights with Strickland, submitting the former champion.

Still, Du Plessis might have to wait for another title shot; fan interest in a third clash with Strickland seems low, while the American’s bout with Chimaev in May was the culmination of a bitter feud between former teammates – meaning a rematch might be a priority for the UFC.

title: Random Ramsdom: What will the Aaron Donald talk be this week?
description: Los Angeles Rams News and Links for 7/20/26
fullText:
Is Aaron Donald coming back to the Los Angeles Rams? Is Aaron Donald not coming back to the Los Angeles Rams? Does anyone know what’s going to happen? I don’t, and I expect another week of speculation, but who knows? Maybe AD makes a decision this week.

It’s big new for the Rams who would seemingly love to have Donald back. Would Donald love to be back? Great question. We’ll see.

In the meantime, enjoy the speculation, enjoy the possibilities. We should have an answer soon enough. Training camp for the Rams starts Saturday so maybe we’ll know by then? Stay tuned.

Thank you for checking out Turf Show Times and have a great Monday!

“On Monday, NFL insider Albert Breer of Sports Illustrated explained why fans shouldn’t yet assume that Donald will be on the Rams’ active roster in September.

“Donald was very happy in retirement, and never ruminated to those in the organization that he wanted to come back,” Breer wrote. “In other words, this isn’t a guy who has had some enormous struggle adjusting to civilian life. The 10-time Pro Bowler is a happy retiree, by all accounts, which is to say the idea of going back to that life isn’t something he’ll view as a failure.”

“Verse is a quality run defender. Nobody disputes that. But it becomes difficult to classify any edge rusher as truly elite when he finishes a healthy season with 4.5 sacks. The analytics community will point to pressures, hurries, and how Verse affects the pocket. Those metrics have value. But the NFL is a results-based business, and the most valuable result a pass rusher can deliver is putting the quarterback on the ground.

A sack is an immediate loss of yards. It moves the chains backward. It helps the defense get off the field, which gives the offense more opportunities to score. A pressure or a forced throw can influence an outcome, but a sack guarantees one.“

“However, despite not having a deal in hand, Nacua remains committed to the goals of this season and he hopes to secure a long-term deal for the future. According to NFL reporter Arye Pulli, when questioned about whether he wants to remain in Los Angeles, Nacua recently stated that staying with the Rams is his intent.

“That’s the plan,” Nacua said at Fanatics Fest.“

“While sixth-rounder CJ Daniels will have every chance to claim the WR3 mantle for himself, he is not the default frontrunner that a first-rounder or a Day 2 pick could have been.

As a result, the team will stage a wide-open melee during training camp and into the preseason to determine who, if anyone, is ready to step up as a third option. The outcome will also provide a verdict as to whether selecting quarterback Ty Simpson with the 13th pick, rather than a win-now contributor, was a mistake that will materially hurt LA’s Super Bowl hopes this season.“

title: Warde Manuel to Step Down as Michigan AD
description: The school announced Warde Manuel is stepping down at the end of the calendar year.
fullText:
On Monday, Michigan president Domenico Grasso announced athletic director Warde Manuel will step down in the wake of multiple scandals at the athletic department, most recently the Sherrone Moore affair. Manuel will stay on until the end of the calendar year.

“I have the deepest respect for Warde and his countless contributions to Michigan, and have asked him to stay on until the end of the calendar year,” Grasso said. He will stay on as a “special advisor to the president” to help “implement” the reforms recommended in the wake of a report into the athletic department’s culture after the Sherrone Moore affair.

It is unclear what Manuel’s financial future will hold. Manuel’s annual base salary is $2.4 million per year, and his contract stipulates he be paid through his term or for 36 months if he is terminated without cause.

The news comes after a tumultuous week for Manuel and Michigan as multiple leaks suggested he was aware of an affair between former head football coach Sherrone Moore and executive assistant Paige Shiver—and did not adequately investigate the situation.

In multiple statements to media members this week, he denied that he had been informed the school was considering firing him, though he implied he had conversations about his future.

Manuel, who has served as athletic director since 2016, was also at the helm during the infamous “sign-stealing scandal,” a cheating scandal where a former coach at Michigan, Conor Stalions, surveilled other teams to steal their signs (the NCAA handed down tens of millions of dollars in penalties). In the middle of this scandal, he received a vote of confidence—in December 2024 he signed the 2030 contract extension.

But Manuel was also leading the department that saw some of the school’s recent wins, including the 2023 football national championship and the 2026 men’s basketball national championship (though neither football coach Jim Harbaugh nor men’s basketball coach Dusty May remain at the program).

The post Warde Manuel to Step Down as Michigan AD appeared first on Front Office Sports.

title: Michigan AD Warde Manuel to step down after calendar year, ending embattled tenure
description: Michigan athletic director Warde Manuel is stepping down after several scandals, including an investigation following Sherrone Moore's firing.
fullText:
Michigan athletic director Warde Manuel will step down following the 2026-27 calendar year amid an outside investigation into the culture of the athletics department, the school announced on Monday.

The investigation began in the wake of former football coach Sherrone Moore’s dismissal last December for having an impermissible relationship with a department staffer and then lying about the relationship.

Manuel had been a focus of the investigation, along with the department-wide culture that contributed to a run of scandals since he was hired in 2016.

REQUIRED READING: Michigan athletic director Warde Manuel future avoided in Board of Regents meeting

"At this time, the university does not have additional public statements planned regarding these matters, nor does the university plan to release any associated documents," a Michigan spokesperson said to The Detroit News' Sarah Atwood. "We can confirm, however, that Director of Athletics Warde Manuel will step down at the end of the calendar year and a search for a new AD will be conducted."

The football program alone has been embroiled in two major scandals under Manuel's watch: Moore’s tumultuous exit after two seasons and the sign-stealing black eye that cast a broad shadow over Michigan’s 2023 national championship under former coach Jim Harbaugh.

Harbaugh was also suspended for three games the same season for recruiting violations and a former assistant coach was indicted in March 2025 for allegedly hacking into the personal accounts of Michigan student-athletes, mostly women, to access intimate photos and videos.

In addition, former hockey coach Mel Pearson was dismissed in 2022 after an investigation revealed a “toxic work environment,” among other issues, and former basketball coach Juwan Howard was suspended in 2022 for striking a Wisconsin assistant coach following a loss to the Badgers.

The athletics department did hit significant highs during Manuel’s tenure, however. The successes include the football championship, five men’s hockey trips to the Frozen Four and this year’s men’s basketball national championship under former coach Dusty May, who has since left for the NBA.

Manuel was given a five-year extension as athletics director in December 2024 with a base salary of $1.9 million. That contract was scheduled to expire in 2030.

This article originally appeared on USA TODAY: Michigan AD Warde Manuel to step down after calendar year, ending embattled tenure

title: Next Men's World Cup set for three continents. What to know about FIFA 2030
description: Come the next World Cup, Spain will defend its current champion title on home soil.
fullText:
In the next edition of the FIFA Men's World Cup, Spain will defend its championship on home soil, as the European country will host World Cup 2030 jointly with Portugal and Morocco.

The next edition of the Men's World Cup will take place in the Mediterranean, particularly in Morocco, Portugal and Spain, the FIFA Council confirmed.

The 2030 tournament will mark the 100 anniversary since the first World Cup was contested in Uruguay in 1930. To commemorate a century of history, FIFA agreed to host a centenary celebration in the Southern Cone of South America, specifically in Paraguay, Uruguay and Argentina. All three countries are a few hours drive away from each other.

One game will be played in each of the South American countries. All other matches will take place in the Mediterranean host countries.

"In 2030 the FIFA World Cup will unite three continents and six countries, inviting the entire world to join in the celebration of the beautiful game," FIFA stated.

The centenary ceremony will take place in Uruguay's capital, Montevideo, five hours away from Argentina's capital, Buenos Aires.

FIFA said that the national soccer team of each of the six host countries will automatically qualify for the tournament.

Saudi Arabia will host the 2034 Men's World Cup across five host cities. The country will be the only hosting nation.

Juan Carlos Castillo is a New Jersey-based trending reporter for the USA Today network. He covers weather, and national events focusing on how they affect New Jersey, Pennsylvania and Delaware.

This article originally appeared on Asbury Park Press: Next Men's World Cup set for three continents. What to know about FIFA 2030

title: ‘I’m A Top Contender’ — Luke Lessei Reasserts ONE World Title Credentials After Rebound Win Over Mohamed Younes Rabah
description: "The Chef" stirred the pot about what comes next after his big win this past Friday.
fullText:
Luke “The Chef” Lessei has always been at his most dangerous when the doubters are loudest, and ONE Fight Night 45 was the most emphatic evidence yet.

Hungry to respond after a recent setback, the Iowa native delivered a commanding unanimous decision over “The Eagle” Mohamed Younes Rabah in the featherweight Muay Thai main event, which aired live in U.S. primetime from Bangkok’s iconic Lumpinee Stadium on Friday, July 17.

Lessei’s striking has always been a genuinely captivating blend of surgical precision and explosive flair, but consistency has sometimes been the missing ingredient.

The painful TKO defeat to now-ONE Interim Featherweight Muay Thai World Champion Nico “King of the North” Carrillo last November raised uncomfortable questions about his place among the division’s elite.

Against Rabah, a sharper, more deliberate Lessei stepped into the ring, looking every bit like a fighter reborn. Behind that renewed composure lay the defining trait of a warrior who never buckles when his back is against the wall.

“It felt great. I’m always happy to get a win. Fun fact, I’ve actually never lost two fights in a row. So I got that streak alive coming off a loss against a champion and a win against a top contender like Younes, who’s one of my friends.”

The opening round did not unfold as Lessei had envisioned, with Rabah finding his range quickly and seizing the advantage with clean, punishing combinations.

Sensing the momentum shifting, the 30-year-old tactician absorbed a crucial corner adjustment that changed the course of the fight entirely.

“My coach started telling me to trust my boxing and double up my hands a little bit more. I started to find my range with the jab, and it just brought me more confidence.

“I realized that the long straight shots were going to win me the rest of that fight, so I was just trusting in my actual reach, trusting in my coach.”

Lessei took full control as the fight progressed, his shots landing with greater frequency and force. A thunderous hook in the final stanza rocked Rabah badly, briefly buckling the Algerian’s knees before his toughness kept him upright.

Although all three judges awarded him the nod, Lessei’s exacting nature craves something far more emphatic. The cerebral finisher wanted something more decisive, and he is already thinking about how to close that gap.

“There was one shot in the third round — a big hook, and my coach tells me like it should have been an eight-count. I wish I had gotten that eight-count. I could have smelled blood and went for the kill, chasing the 50K bonus.

“But it is what it is. I’m happy with the shots that I landed. I can always improve. I can always do better, and I think I could push the pace even more.”

Luke Lessei’s win over Mohamed Younes Rabah means far more than a return to form, thrusting him firmly back into featherweight contention.

Looking ahead, three names stand out. A rematch with Nico Carrillo tops the list, while Bampara Kouyate, who handed him a buzzer-beater knockout, remains unforgotten. The American striker also remains deeply intrigued by a potential showdown with Thai standout Shadow Mavinn.

Lessei’s desire to test his mettle against the division’s best burns so deeply that he would gladly do so under kickboxing rules if need be:

“I just heard [Shadow] has a broken jaw right now, so I’m not sure how soon he’ll be back, but all three of the names — Bambi, Nico, Shadow, whoever.

“I’ve always been interested in putting the big gloves back on, doing kickboxing, since there’s no big-glove Muay Thai. But, yeah, I love banging in whatever style it is for striking.”

With reigning ONE Featherweight Muay Thai World Champion Tawanchai PK Saenchai sidelined through injury, the division is ripe with opportunity — and Lessei has every intention of seizing it.

For now, “The Chef” is savoring Friday night’s result. The 26 pounds of gold remain the ultimate destination, and every performance is another step toward it.

“A chance to fight for the belt is always at the top of the list, so I can’t really decide. I’m just down to fight whoever. I just love fighting for ONE. I love performing for my fans.

“I want to make sure I enjoy my win, and I focus on being present, but I think I’m a top contender. I want to keep reminding people with good performances.”

title: Barcelona expecting a public gesture from priority target with World Cup over
description: The FIFA World Cup final was all that was hindering progress in the Barcelona-Julian Alvarez saga, for the player was an integral member of Lionel Scaloni’s side and did not wish to cause any distur...
fullText:
The FIFA World Cup final was all that was hindering progress in the Barcelona-Julian Alvarez saga, for the player was an integral member of Lionel Scaloni’s side and did not wish to cause any disturbance in the dressing room during the tournament.

In recent days, bridges between Alvarez and Atletico Madrid took a big hit, especially after the Spanish club’s CEO openly declared that the player was not for sale at any price.

He openly called out Barcelona and Joan Laporta for their approaches and maintained that the player would not leave, thereby breaking a promise they made to the player last season.

As reported by SPORT now, the moment of truth has now arrived in the Alvarez saga now that the World Cup is complete.

Alvarez must now take a stand. (Photo by Elsa/Getty Images)

Alvarez is furious with Atletico Madrid for going back on their word, for they had promised to enable a departure should the player desire it at the end of the season.

Now that the international tournament is complete, there is an expectation within Barcelona that the Argentine is willing to step forward and make a move from his side.

The ball is now in Alvarez’s court, and while a departure is still complicated, a public announcement of his desire to leave and play for Barcelona can change a lot.

It will put pressure on Diego Simeone’s side and force the negotiating doors open.

If the Spanish outlet is to be believed, Alvarez wishes to clarify a lot of hidden truths about his situation and how he has been wronged. The time has now arrived, and Barcelona are expecting the said gesture from his side.

Barcelona will not wait for Alvarez forever. (Photo by Eric Alonso/Getty Images)

The World Cup eclipsed a major part of the ongoing summer transfer window, and while it is now past us, so is the majority of the negotiating window clubs had.

Barcelona are thus looking to be smart about its situation and do not wish to throw all their eggs in one basket.

The Argentine striker is the dream signing, but the board have reportedly set a deadline of 31st July to decide on his transfer.

Should no gesture arrive and Atletico Madrid continue to keep a firm stance, they will start looking for alternative options.

title: 'The Era of Red, Blue & Gold!': IPL champions RCB shares unique congratulatory message for FIFA World Cup winners Spain
description: With Spain lifting the FIFA World Cup yesterday, current and back-to-back IPL champions, RCB shared a unique congratulating message for the world champions.
fullText:
'The Era of Red, Blue & Gold!': IPL champions RCB shares unique congratulatory message for FIFA World Cup winners Spain originally appeared on Cricket News. Add Cricket News as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

RCB congratulated Spain on their FIFA World Cup triumph with a themed post.

Ferran Torres scored in extra time as Spain beat Argentina 1-0 in New Jersey.

Both RCB and Spain have now completed back-to-back major title successes.

Spain were crowned world champions on Sunday night, with substitute Ferran Torres striking in the 106th minute to seal a 1-0 win over Argentina at the New York New Jersey Stadium in East Rutherford.

It was a final Spain dominated throughout, managing 20 shots to Argentina's two and taking the game away from a side reduced to 10 men after Enzo Fernandez was sent off for a second bookable offence in stoppage time.

The tributes arrived from every corner of the world of sport, and among the more unusual came from Indian cricket. Royal Challengers Bengaluru, the back-to-back IPL champions, posted a message linking their own success with Spain's.

MORE: Andy Flower committed to RCB: IPL-winning coach explains why he has ruled himself out of England coaching role

The franchise shared an image combining Spain's celebrations with their own title-winning moments from 2025 and 2026, drawing a line between two teams who have each retained their status at the top.

"Congratulations to Spain on winning the FIFA WC. The Era of Red, Blue & Gold!" the RCB account posted on X.

Congratulations to Spain on winning the FIFA WC. The Era of Red, Blue & Gold! ⭐️🏆#PlayBold#ನಮ್ಮRCBpic.twitter.com/1wKxZw0dcR

— Royal Challengers Bengaluru (@RCBTweets) July 20, 2026

The colour reference nods to the red and gold that both Spain and RCB carry, while the blue points to the Barcelona core running through this Spain side, with Torres, Lamine Yamal and Pau Cubarsi all products of the Catalan club.

The parallel between the two sides holds up. RCB ended an 18-year wait for a maiden IPL title in 2025 before defending it in 2026, while Spain followed their Euro 2024 triumph with a second World Cup crown, 16 years after their first in 2010.

MORE: Full list of injuries suffered by India stars in 2026 as concerns of fitness raised

Franchise accounts posting about other sports usually feel forced, but this one has a genuine thread running through it. Both teams spent years being described as talented underachievers before finally converting that potential into silverware.

The comparison also lands on the method rather than just the results. Spain won this tournament by control, conceding a single goal across the entire competition, while RCB's back-to-back titles were built on Andy Flower turning a batting-heavy side into a balanced one.

There is a commercial logic, too. RCB has the largest social following of any IPL franchise, and a World Cup final watched by hundreds of millions offers an obvious moment to reach beyond cricket, particularly with Indian audiences increasingly following European football.

title: Calcutta High Court orders immediate arrest of Delhi Capitals and Bengal wicket-keeper Abhishek Porel
description: Things go from bad to worse for the Delhi Capitals after primary wicketkeeper and Bengal Ranji star Abhishek Porel is ordered to be immediately arrested by Calcutta High Court.
fullText:
Calcutta High Court orders immediate arrest of Delhi Capitals and Bengal wicket-keeper Abhishek Porel originally appeared on Cricket News. Add Cricket News as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

The Calcutta High Court has directed police to arrest cricketer Abhishek Porel immediately.

The court also ordered the seizure of his electronic devices to protect the complainant.

Porel has denied all allegations against him since the FIR was registered in June.

Indian cricket has been rocked by a serious legal development involving one of its brightest young talents. The Calcutta High Court has directed police to immediately arrest Delhi Capitals and Bengal wicketkeeper Abhishek Porel in connection with an alleged rape case.

According to Live Law, the court has also ordered authorities to seize the 23-year-old's electronic devices, a step taken specifically to protect the privacy and dignity of the complainant in the matter.

The case has been building since June, when a complaint was first registered against the cricketer in West Bengal. Porel, who has represented Delhi Capitals since 2023, has consistently denied the allegations made against him.

MORE: ''If there's no noise, there's no fun': Rohit brushes off ODI retirement speculation

The directions relate to Mogra Police Station FIR No. 346 of 2026, which Live Law reports includes several non-bailable offences under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, among them rape and criminal intimidation, alongside sections of the Information Technology Act.

#BREAKING The Calcutta High Court has directed police to immediately arrest #Bengal & Delhi Capitals cricketer Abishek Porel and seize his electronic devices in an alleged rape case, where Porel has been accused of threatening to circulate intimate photos and videos of a medical… pic.twitter.com/pVKq0rjtjq

— Live Law (@LiveLawIndia) July 20, 2026

Porel has been accused of allegedly threatening to circulate intimate photographs and videos of a medical student, who is the complainant in the case. The court petition submitted that further steps were required by the police to seize devices allegedly containing obscene photographs of the complainant.

Journalist Subhayan Chakraborty of RevSportz, who has followed the case since its inception, reported that the court specifically instructed authorities to seize the devices in order to protect the complainant's privacy and prevent the sharing of any such material.

The original complaint was lodged at Mogra Police Station in the Hooghly district by the complainant and her mother in June, weeks after the conclusion of IPL 2026. It was alleged that the two had been in a relationship for over three years.

Porel denied the allegations when they first surfaced. "I am playing well now, so things are coming up. Will talk about this when police investigate," the cricketer told the media at the time, dismissing the claims as false.

MORE: Ro-Ko serve timely reminder to India selectors

It must be stressed that these remain allegations, and Porel is entitled to the presumption of innocence until any court determines otherwise. A direction to arrest is a procedural step in an investigation rather than a finding of guilt.

The professional consequences, however, are already significant. Porel was retained by Delhi Capitals for four crore rupees ahead of IPL 2026 and has been a fixture in Bengal's domestic setup, and non-bailable charges of this nature inevitably raise questions about his immediate availability.

Neither Delhi Capitals nor the Cricket Association of Bengal has issued a public statement. Indian cricket has faced similar situations recently, with Royal Challengers Bengaluru's Yash Dayal missing an entire IPL season while a comparable case remained unresolved, and the response to this one will be closely watched.

title: Agency carrying out early-morning doping tests on the Tour says correct procedures were followed
description: The agency carrying out early-morning anti-doping tests which left Tour de France riders frustrated maintains that correct procedures were followed.  Defending Tour champion Tadej Pogacar, the current race leader, and two-time winner Jonas Vingegaard were woken in the early hours of Sunday by officials for unannounced tests.  Pogacar said his test was carried out at 5 a.m. and that Vingegaard’s was at 2 a.
fullText:
PARIS (AP) — The agency carrying out early-morning anti-doping tests which left Tour de France riders frustrated maintains that correct procedures were followed.

Defending Tour champion Tadej Pogacar, the current race leader, and two-time winner Jonas Vingegaard were woken in the early hours of Sunday by officials for unannounced tests. Pogacar said his test was carried out at 5 a.m. and that Vingegaard’s was at 2 a.m.

Vingegaard crashed near the end of Sunday's 15th stage, sustaining a fractured collarbone which forced the Danish rider to abandon the race.

Pogacar and stage winner Remco Evenepoel were both critical of the tests being done so early. Cycling’s governing body, the UCI, has entrusted its Tour testing to the Swiss-based International Testing Agency since 2021.

“The ITA is conscious that nighttime testing may be disruptive to riders’ rest and recovery,” the ITA said in an email to The Associated Press on Monday. “However, the ITA, on behalf of the UCI, has the responsibility to ensure the effectiveness of the cycling anti-doping program and the integrity of cycling races.”

Evenepoel said in his post-stage news conference that it was “very disrespectful” to wake riders up so early and called on cycling riders' association CPA to respond. CPA did not immediately respond to a request from comment.

Four-time Tour winner Pogacar took exception to how early Vingegaard's test was, suggesting that fatigue may have played a factor in his crash. However, the Slovenian star later said random-hour testing was valid if it improves transparency.

“I accept it, if this is necessary to still prove we are riding clean,” he was quoted as saying by the cyclingnews.com website. “If this is necessary then I totally accept it and I commit to this. But it’s a bit (expletive) when you sleep for four hours in one day in the middle of a three-week Tour.”

The ITA said it acted within the parameters of what is allowed.

“Effective testing must, in limited and justified circumstances, be capable to occur outside standard daytime hours,” the ITA explained, adding that testing is subject to safeguards established under the World Anti-Doping Code, UCI anti-doping rules and French law.

But some observers questioned why Pogacar and Vingegaard — who are world-leading riders — were tested on the same day and so early in the morning.

“The timing of a control is determined through a risk-based assessment that takes into account a range of relevant factors and information,” the ITA said. “But nor should it be interpreted as evidence that a rider has committed, or is suspected of having committed, an anti-doping rule violation.”

Analyzing a sample taken from a rider can take several weeks, the ITA said. The race ends July 26 in Paris. ___

AP sports: https://apnews.com/sports

title: USC opposing player scouting report - Dezmen Roebuck, Washington
description: Dezmen Roebuck was good in 2025. USC has to make sure the Washington Huskies' emerging receiver isn't great in 2026
fullText:
USC football plays Washington this season. UW Huskies Wire looked at Washington receiver Dezmen Roebuck.

"A new number isn't the only thing Dezmen Roebuck has going for him this season, as the Washington Huskies' sophomore standout will also be shifting from the Z position inside to the slot after a stellar first year on Montlake. The former three-star recruit from Marana High School in Arizona burst onto the scene during fall camp ahead of his freshman year and made himself indispensable to Jedd Fisch's coaching staff en route to a Freshman All-America campaign where he hauled in 42 catches for 560 yards and a freshman program record 7 touchdowns. Now, with more room to operate in the slot, the sure-handed pass catcher feels like he has a little more room to do as he pleases over the middle.

"'I feel like I definitely have more freedom to do what I need to do to get open,' Roebuck said of the position change. 'I feel like outside, it was more technical, so you get a little more freedom.'

"Throughout his freshman year, Roebuck showed off an advanced skill set for his age, with one of his best traits being his feel for opposing defenses, constantly finding soft spots in zones, which helped turn him into a reliable security blanket for his youth teammate, quarterback Demond Williams Jr.

"'I feel like it's a little bit of everything,' he said of his ability to get open. 'You've obviously got to take time out of your day to study with the coaches, study with the quarterbacks, study with other receivers in the room just so you are all on the same page and you have the quarterback's trust, but I also feel like I have a good feel for the game. I know a lot, and I've been through a lot of stuff. I played early, last year I played in a lot of games, so just knowing what I've got to do and where I've got to get to get open.'"

This article originally appeared on Trojans Wire: USC opposing player scouting report - Dezmen Roebuck, Washington

title: Has LeBron James made a decision yet? Lebron James next team odds
description: The Philadelphia 76ers are among a handful of teams making a pitch to sign free the NBA legend. See Lebron James next team odds.
fullText:
Do the Philadelphia 76ers really have a shot?

Following the announcement he wouldn't re-sign with the Los Angeles Lakers, NBA legend LeBron James has made it clear to teams that he would consider signing with a franchise that best fit his needs.

What those are aren't exactly clear, but the Sixers are reportedly one of the teams in the mix for James' services for the upcoming 2026-2027 NBA season — and possibly beyond.

See where the Sixers currently stand among James' suitors.

As of July 20, the Sixers ranked among the top possible teams to sign the all-time NBA leading scorer, according to prediction market Kalshi.

Here's where they rank as of 9:25 a.m. ET July 20:

Cleveland Cavaliers 33% (up 4%)

Golden State Warriors 13% (down 1%)

Here's the breakdown of James' current odds of signing, according to market prediction site Polymarket:

Cleveland Cavaliers 31% (down 10%)

Golden State Warriors 10% (down 16%)

While most believe James will return to his hometown Cleveland to close out his career, there's a chance a he turns down a third reunion with the Cavs and signs elsewhere.

Recently, James' agent Rich Paul of Klutch Sports discussed a number of teams his client was considering on his “Game Over” podcast with Max Kellerman.

On a white board, Paul and Kellerman went through the pros of James signing with each team and their starting rosters. The list included:

According to a report by ESPN's Shams Charania, those teams seemed to have been cut down to the Cavaliers, Heat, 76ers and Warriors.

Charania said executives for interested teams have been sending voice notes to Paul, who passed them along to James.

“There's a focus on Cleveland, Miami and Philadelphia," Charania said in a July 14 report. " ... This could be any day, any week, now," but that James was getting closer to a decision.

@ShamsCharania latest update on LeBron James' free agency 👑

“There's a focus on Cleveland, Miami and Philadelphia." pic.twitter.com/0P3Cv0F93O

According to Kalshi, here's when James is expected to sign with a new team:

While appearing with 97.5 The Fanantic in Philadelphia last week, ESPN's Dave McMenamin said it was likely that James would make his decision this week.

"A source familiar with LeBron really believes LeBron is nearing the decision ... People around the league are hoping that it comes next week," McMenamin said.

"... I would say there is a pretty decent chance we find out next week. I do believe, strongly, that the Sixers are a legitimate option for him still. I was told, as of last night, he still hasn't made his final decision."

Speculation that the Heat are now the frontrunners for James' services after Miami jumped ahead of Cleveland in the prediction markets over the weekend.

During a July 16 report, Charania said James' decision was expected soon.

"I think this process is getting closer and closer to the end here," Charania said. "LeBron James has all the information that he needs."

BREAKING: LeBron James is rumoured to be signing with a team VERY soon, per @ShamsCharania.

📈 Shams says that LeBron, after facing criticism for taking too long, has all the information to make a final choice.

There are rumours that LeBron could be doing a "big event" for it. pic.twitter.com/6KK2T8udZN

— BallersCulture (@BallersCultureX) July 17, 2026

Reports are speculating that James could announce his signing during Fanatics Fest, which is being held this week in at the Javits Center in New York.

During the July 16 Fanatics event on July 17, James declined to say if had made a free-agent decision yet.

Tyrese Haliburton to LeBron James: 

“What are you going on, what is this… Year 76?”

I see what you did there 😏 pic.twitter.com/RMv5kzYeMw

— RB (@RBPhillyTake) July 16, 2026

"I won't hold you guys up too much longer," James said.

During his Fanatics appearance, James even said the phrase "trust the process," which Sixers fans were quick to connect to the Sixers' longtime rebuilding era with Embiid.

While appearing on the Pat McAfee Show on July 16, Charania reported that “I don’t know if I’m holding my breath for an announcement this week."

He is also scheduled to tape an episode of "The Shop," focusing on basketball, business and entrepreneurship at noon ET on July 17.

As LeBron James rattles off what he will value in his next team, he used the phrase “trust the process” and the crowd was sent into a tizzy, thinking he was giving a clue about the Philadelphia 76ers. pic.twitter.com/klpBWePWNq

— Dave McMenamin (@mcten) July 17, 2026

Another report over the weekend stated that Sixers owner Josh Harris also spoke to James briefly during Fanatics Fest.

During his appearance on 97.5 The Fanantic in Philadelphia, McMenamin said it's top Sixers players Joel Embiid, Tyrese Maxey and newly acquired forward Jaylen Brown who are currently in contact with James. and not Philly brass such as Bob Meyers, Mike Gansey and Nick Nurse.

"I want to say the people that I've spoken to told me about (Joel) Embiid's interaction, it's been mostly through text messaging, which is an obvious way to communicate in 2026," McMenamin said. "As far as my understanding, it's really been (Tyrese) Maxey as the primary spearhead in terms of keeping that communication and dialogue going. Maxey knows LeBron the best out of those three players

"… I've covered a bunch of teams, and it's not very often where you get all three, if you do have three stars, on the same page moving in the same direction," he said. "It's significant that, from the top down, Bob Myers, Nick Nurse, Mike Gansey, and the three best players on the team are all together on this, saying, 'LeBron, we want you, we need you, and we can do great things if you come here.'"

If James were to sign with Philadelphia, the Sixers would potentially have one of the most talented starting lineups in the NBA. Here's what it might look it:

Sixers potential starting lineup

This article originally appeared on Delaware News Journal: Has LeBron James made a decision yet? Lebron James next team odds

title: Michigan Confirms Warde Manuel Will Step Down as Athletic Director
description: The University of Michigan has confirmed that Athletic Director Warde Manuel will step down at the end of the 2026 calendar year and that a search for a new AD will begin.
fullText:
The speculation surrounding Michigan Athletic Director Warde Manuel has come to an end.

According to a statement shared by University of Michigan spokesperson Paul Corliss, Manuel will step down as athletic director at the end of the 2026 calendar year, with a national search set to begin for his successor.

The university has not announced a replacement or provided additional details regarding the transition.

The statement confirms that Manuel will remain in his role through the end of the year before officially stepping aside.

The university’s statement reads:

“At this time, the university does not have any additional public statements planned regarding these matters, nor does the university plan to release any associated documents. We can confirm, however, that Director of Athletics Warde Manuel will step down at the end of the calendar year and a search for a new AD will be conducted.”

Manuel has served as Michigan’s athletic director since 2016, overseeing one of the most successful eras in the department’s history.

During his tenure, Michigan celebrated national championships across multiple sports, including football and men’s basketball, while capturing numerous Big Ten titles and maintaining one of the nation’s premier athletic departments.

At the same time, his administration also navigated several high-profile controversies, including the Connor Stallions sign-stealing scandal, NCAA investigations involving the football program, and the investigation into former football coach Sherrone Moore.

Michigan has not identified a timetable for naming Manuel’s successor, but the university confirmed that a search for a new athletic director will begin.

With Manuel remaining in his position through the end of the calendar year, the Wolverines now have several months to conduct one of the most significant leadership searches in college athletics.

More information is expected as the university begins the process of identifying the next leader of Michigan Athletics.

title: Inter star Dimarco picks out Scudetto rivals and reveals key to fighting complacency
description: Federico Dimarco has insisted Inter will approach the new season with the same hunger that delivered last term’s Double, while identifying a crowded field of Scudetto challengers.According to TuttoM...
fullText:
Federico Dimarco has insisted Inter will approach the new season with the same hunger that delivered last term’s Double, while identifying a crowded field of Scudetto challengers.

According to TuttoMercatoWeb, the left-back, who provided 18 assists and was named Serie A MVP last season, is refusing to let the weight of expectation change his approach.

“I always try to do my bit and give the team a big hand. My way of training hasn’t changed, not after the Champions League disappointment nor after last year’s Double,” Dimarco said. “Sometimes results come more, sometimes a little less, but to have a clear conscience you must always give 100%. That’s the basis for continuing to grow day by day.”

UDINE, ITALY – OCTOBER 14: Federico Dimarco of Italy looks on during the warm up prior to the FIFA World Cup 2026 qualifier match between Italy and Israel at Stadio Friuli on October 14, 2025 in Udine, Italy. (Photo by Marco Luzzani/Getty Images)

With two trophies in the bank, Dimarco was clear about the danger of complacency and how to guard against it.

“You fight it by training well, with desire and enthusiasm, following the coach’s demands and, above all, keeping the hunger to win,” he said. “That’s the most important thing: if you’re hungry to win, you already start a step ahead.”

As for the title race itself, the Italy international sees a competitive division and even flagged a possible edge for those outside Europe.

“I see this Inter as in other years: a team that sets out to fight to win the Scudetto. We’ve never hidden from that, even when we didn’t manage it in the past,” he said.

“I think all the teams start at the same level, though those not playing in the Champions League may have a small advantage, because that competition takes away a lot of energy. The main rivals will be Inter, Milan, Napoli, Juventus, Roma and Atalanta. Como are strengthening too. There are many teams that can have their say across the whole season.”

title: Ballon d’Or 2026 odds: Harry Kane leads the betting after World Cup final
description: The Ballon d’Or race has taken another dramatic turn following the conclusion of the 2026 World Cup, with Harry Kane now heading the betting after another remarkable campaign for club and country.Sp...
fullText:
The Ballon d’Or race has taken another dramatic turn following the conclusion of the 2026 World Cup, with Harry Kane now heading the betting after another remarkable campaign for club and country.

Spain’s World Cup triumph has also seen Lamine Yamal and Rodri shorten significantly in the market after starring throughout the tournament, while Lionel Messi’s run to a second consecutive World Cup final keeps the Argentine firmly among the favourites.

Below, we assess the leading contenders in the latest Ballon d’Or betting.

Harry Kane has moved to the head of the market after arguably the finest season of his career.

The England captain finally ended his wait for major silverware by helping Bayern Munich to another Bundesliga title before leading the Three Lions to the World Cup semi-finals. Kane once again finished among Europe’s leading goalscorers, combining relentless finishing with his trademark creativity.

Although England fell short against Argentina, Kane’s consistency across the entire campaign leaves him as the bookmaker’s favourite for football’s biggest individual prize.

Lamine Yamal continues to rewrite the record books.

Still only a teenager, the Barcelona winger inspired Spain to World Cup glory with another series of match-winning performances on the biggest stage. His creativity, dribbling and composure regularly separated him from defenders throughout the tournament, cementing his status as one of the world’s elite attackers.

Winning the World Cup has propelled Yamal into serious Ballon d’Or contention, and few players possess stronger momentum heading into the final months of voting.

Lionel Messi was priced at 50/1 to win the Ballon d’Or when the 2026 World Cup got underway, but his stunning performances for Argentina have dramatically changed the picture.

The 39-year-old has scored eight goals in six matches so far, inspiring the defending champions to another World Cup Final, and shrunk his Ballon d’Or odds to 4/1.

The Argentina captain guided the reigning champions to a second successive World Cup final and once again produced decisive moments throughout the tournament. His creativity remained central to Lionel Scaloni’s side, while his influence on and off the pitch reinforced his standing as one of the game’s greatest players.

Although Argentina narrowly missed out on retaining their title, Messi’s performances ensure he remains firmly in the Ballon d’Or conversation.

Rodri reminded everyone why he remains one of football’s most influential players.

The Manchester City midfielder controlled games throughout Spain’s victorious World Cup campaign and capped another outstanding year by lifting the biggest trophy in international football. His ability to dictate possession, break up attacks and deliver in decisive moments makes him one of the leading candidates.

Few midfielders combine consistency and silverware as effectively as Rodri, making 4/1 a fair reflection of his credentials.

Kylian Mbappe rarely stays out of Ballon d’Or discussions for long.

The France captain enjoyed another prolific season at club level and continued to deliver on the international stage despite Les Bleus falling short of another World Cup final. His pace, finishing and ability to decide the biggest matches keep him among the leading contenders. Mbappe became the all-time World Cup top goalscorer this summer and now has 22 goals to his name.

Another strong domestic campaign means Mbappe cannot be discounted despite drifting slightly in the betting.

Jude Bellingham (20/1) enjoyed another excellent campaign despite England’s semi-final exit, while Ousmane Dembele (20/1) remains an outsider after another impressive season.

Fabian Ruiz (25/1) enhanced his reputation during Spain’s successful World Cup campaign, with Michael Olise (25/1) also attracting support following an outstanding breakthrough year for club and country.

Erling Haaland (33/1) will hope another fast start to the season keeps him in contention, while Khvicha Kvaratskhelia (33/1) and Pau Cubarsi (33/1) complete the current list of leading outsiders

title: 49ers stars vouch for return of former WR in free agency
description: One former 49ers wideout remains a free agent.
fullText:
The San Francisco 49ers made a couple of moves at wide receiver this offseason, signing Mike Evans and Christian Kirk in free agency, while drafting De’Zhaun Stribling with their first pick of the 2026 NFL Draft at No. 33 overall.

Those three additions provide much-needed depth and talent at wide receiver, where the 49ers struggled in 2025. Without Brandon Aiyuk and Deebo Samuel, the 49ers didn’t have a wideout top 650 receiving yards, as injuries and struggles on the field led the way.

Evans gives the 49ers a proven No. 1 wide receiver who can win on the outside, although he comes with his own risks after the most injury-laden season of his career in 2025. He’ll be 33 this season, but has been highly productive when healthy and should be a good red-zone weapon.

Kirk dealt with injuries as well in 2025, but is another proven weapon who can operate out of the slot and give the 49ers good depth alongside Evans and Ricky Pearsall. Stribling’s role as a rookie is unclear, but he worked well after the catch in college and gives the 49ers a big, fast receiver at 225 pounds.

Still, could the 49ers add one more weapon to the room? Former 49ers star Deebo Samuel remains a free agent this offseason after his deal with the Washington Commanders expired, and he’s searching for a new home.

Samuel, 30, caught 72 passes for 727 yards and five touchdowns with the Commanders in 2025, while playing in 16 games. He didn’t see much usage as a runner, getting only 17 carries for 75 yards and a touchdown.

Samuel’s average depth of target was quite low once again at 5.2 yards per target, but he did have some big games for the Commanders, especially early in the season.

Could he be another player that returns to the 49ers on a one-year deal? Some of his former teammates vouched for a potential return this weekend.

On one of Samuel’s recent Instagram posts, both George Kittle and Kyle Juszczyk commented, welcoming the wideout back to the Bay Area should he choose to return.

“I have a guest house with your name on it,” Juszczyk wrote, with Kittle writing a similar remark: ‘I also have a guest house labeled ‘Deebo’s second home.’”

Now, the 49ers have mentioned the desire to get younger and cheaper, but they’ve also added several veterans this offseason, including Evans, Kirk, linebacker Dre Greenlaw, and others, as they look to maximize their current championship window.

Samuel could be another low-cost, one-year option that gives the 49ers more depth and reliability at wide receiver with a familiar face. The wideout should find a new home, should he choose, before the season starts, and there are many better situations for more playing time, including with the Commanders, who don’t have any proven options behind Terry McLaurin.

But there are also options available back home.

title: Ricci offered to Juventus and Atalanta as Amorim reshapes Milan midfield
description: Samuele Ricci could be on his way out of Milan just a year after arriving, with the midfielder not considered a natural fit for Ruben Amorim’s system.According to Matteo Moretto, MilanNews24, and Ca...
fullText:
Samuele Ricci could be on his way out of Milan just a year after arriving, with the midfielder not considered a natural fit for Ruben Amorim’s system.

According to Matteo Moretto, MilanNews24, and Calciomercato.com, Ricci does not figure among Amorim’s tactical priorities, with the Portuguese coach favouring a different profile of player for his two-man midfield.

It is not a question of form, the former Torino talent enjoyed a solid debut campaign of 31 appearances, a goal and four assists despite sitting behind Adrien Rabiot and Luka Modric in the pecking order, but rather one of style, and Milan are weighing a permanent sale to raise funds and reinvest.

HONG KONG, CHINA – JULY 26: Samuele Ricci of AC Milan looks on during the Liverpool FC v AC Milan Pre-Season Friendly match at Kai Tak Stadium on July 26, 2025 in Hong Kong, China. (Photo by Yu Chun Christopher Wong/Eurasia Sport Images/Getty Images)

The player’s representatives have already moved to test the market, with recent contacts reported with both Juventus and Atalanta.

The Bianconeri, whom Giovanni Carnevali and Frederic Massara are building with a more Italian core, have long admired Ricci, his name surfaced in a possible swap for Federico Gatti in the winter window.

At Atalanta, meanwhile, he would have a powerful backer in Maurizio Sarri, who repeatedly sought the midfielder during his time at Lazio.

The logic is partly one of numbers. Amorim wants to avoid overcrowding in the middle, where Milan currently have seven players for two roles, too many even with a return to European football via the Europa League next season.

Signed for €23 million last summer and under contract until 2029, Ricci will not be sold cheaply: Milan want offers starting at around €20 million before they open talks.

Ricci, for his part, is said to be working seriously through pre-season with the clear aim of changing Amorim’s mind. Whether he can force his way into the coach’s plans, or becomes the next piece of Milan’s sell-to-build summer, is a question the coming weeks will answer.

title: Why are Premier League transfer fees soaring?
description: With Morgan Rogers set to become the third £100m-plus Premier League transfer of the summer, BBC Sport looks at how the market keeps growing.
fullText:
Thirty-four years ago a £13m transfer caused national outrage in Italy.

When AC Milan signed Gianluigi Lentini from Torino in 1992 for a then world record fee, newspapers called the deal "obscene", while the Vatican's official newspaper described it as "an offence against the dignity of work".

Today that fee would barely register among the Premier League's biggest transfers.

This summer alone Chelsea have agreed a club record £117m deal for Aston Villa's Morgan Rogers, Manchester City paid Nottingham Forest £116m for Elliot Anderson, and Tottenham spent a club record £100m on Sandro Tonali.

These are not the only major deals. Tottenham also agreed an £85m move for West Ham midfielder Mateus Fernandes, Barcelona paid £69m to sign Anthony Gordon from Newcastle United, and Manchester United spent £50m on Chelsea midfielder Andrey Santos.

It is the first transfer window in Premier League history to produce three £100m-plus signings - and there are still weeks to go before deadline day on 1 September.

During the 2025 summer window English top-flight clubs spent a record £3bn.

So why are transfer fees continuing to climb? Why are clubs paying record sums for players already playing in England? And why are more clubs willing to sell some of their biggest stars?

Rogers' move perhaps best illustrates how today's market works.

Chelsea are buying a player from Villa, who have finished above them in three of the past four seasons. Traditionally, clubs might have been reluctant to strengthen a direct rival in those circumstances.

Instead, Chelsea simply met Villa's valuation.

Football finance expert Kieran Maguire says one record fee quickly influences the next.

"It sets a benchmark," he says. "Forest wanted to be close to the record level because they felt Elliot Anderson was a critical player, a player very much in demand.

"We've then seen that surpassed by the Morgan Rogers fee - only slightly - but it allows the club to say, 'This is the record English signing'. So there's an element of bragging rights involved."

After City's £116m move for Anderson established a new market rate, Villa made it clear any club wanting Rogers would need to exceed that figure. Chelsea did exactly that, agreeing a guaranteed £117m fee.

Arsenal, who had identified Rogers as one of their leading targets, explored a deal but refused to meet Villa's asking price.

The episode illustrates how quickly the market can move. Once a new benchmark is established, comparable players inevitably see their valuations rise.

Part of the answer lies in supply and demand.

Premier League clubs must register at least eight homegrown players in their 25-man squads, while clubs competing in Uefa competitions must also satisfy homegrown quotas. That creates fierce competition for a relatively small pool of players capable of performing consistently at the highest level.

Buying domestically also removes many of the uncertainties associated with overseas recruitment.

Maguire describes Premier League players as "oven-ready".

"Because the Premier League is so wealthy, it's relatively rare that a club is in financial straits where it has to sell," he says.

"In the case of English players, you've then got the homegrown rule, which gives clubs more flexibility.

"You put all of those factors together and it means the Premier League is a big driver."

Clubs know how these players perform against top opposition, and understand they can cope with the physical demands of the Premier League and avoid many of the adaptation risks attached to recruiting from abroad.

That certainty carries a premium. So too does the Premier League's financial strength. Lucrative broadcasting revenues give English clubs spending power unmatched elsewhere in world football, allowing selling clubs to demand higher fees from domestic rivals than they might receive from overseas buyers.

Perhaps the more surprising trend has been clubs allowing key players to leave.

Villa have sold Rogers despite his importance under Unai Emery, while Forest accepted City's offer for Anderson. Newcastle sanctioned Gordon's move despite the England winger becoming one of Eddie Howe's most influential attacking players.

Such decisions reflect commercial strategy as much as football.

Major transfer fees create opportunities to strengthen several areas of a squad rather than relying on one star player, while major sales also provide greater flexibility.

Why are Man City breaking the bank for Anderson?

Those sales have also reignited debate about whether the Premier League's financial regulations place greater pressure on ambitious clubs outside the traditional biggest spenders to sell valuable assets.

Some argue that the rules favour clubs with the greatest financial resources, which are better placed to absorb record transfer fees.

The latest changes are also shaping how clubs approach the transfer market.

Maguire says financial regulations can influence transfer decisions, although he cautions that they are not always the decisive factor.

"Financial regulations might be a driving force, but not necessarily," he says.

"Because clubs don't have to sell from a financial point of view, it means they can extract maximum value from their players."

Villa's sale of Rogers and Forest's decision to cash in on Anderson have become part of that wider discussion, illustrating how regulation now shapes recruitment and squad building across the Premier League.

Rather than viewing these deals simply as losing important players, clubs now see them as opportunities to reshape their squads.

Whether those replacements prove as successful is another matter.

This summer has also brought a significant regulatory change.

The Premier League is transitioning away from profitability and sustainability rules (PSR) towards a new financial framework centred on squad-cost ratio (SCR) and sustainability and systemic resilience (SSR).

Rather than focusing primarily on rolling three-year losses, the new framework places greater emphasis on the relationship between football-related spending and revenue.

Player wages, transfer amortisation – spreading a transfer fee across the length of a player's contract for accounting purposes – and agent fees now become even more central to clubs' financial planning.

For clubs competing in Europe, Uefa's own SCR rules also continue to apply, placing further restrictions on spending.

Why SCR will allow Spurs to keep spending - analysis

Maguire says those accounting rules mean clubs assess transfers very differently from supporters.

"If you're signing a player for £115m, spread over five years that's about £23m a year before wages," he says.

"Getting into the Champions League is worth a fortune and that's the lens through which clubs do those calculations."

Maguire estimates reaching Europe's premier club competition can be worth 180m-200m euros (£153m-£170m) once prize money, matchday revenue and commercial bonuses are taken into account, reinforcing the incentive for clubs to invest heavily in recruitment.

Clubs are already beginning to adapt their transfer strategies. Generating transfer income, reducing wage commitments and carefully balancing spending throughout the year have become key considerations alongside purely footballing decisions.

The Premier League's financial landscape has changed dramatically over the past decade. Since 2016 total summer transfer spending has risen from £1.16bn to a record £3.19bn in 2025 – an increase of almost 175%.

On the surface, that suggests transfer figures are continuing to accelerate. Headline fees, though, tell only part of the story.

Research by Maguire and colleagues at the University of Liverpool suggests some of the Premier League's earlier record signings represented even greater investments when measured against clubs' buying power at the time.

Using Premier League broadcasting revenues as an index, Alan Shearer's £15m move from Blackburn Rovers to Newcastle United in 1996 remains the league's biggest transfer relative to clubs' spending power. Rio Ferdinand's £29.1m move to Manchester United in 2002 and Juan Sebastian Veron's £28.1m arrival at Old Trafford in 2001 also rank above any of this summer's blockbuster deals.

The same model suggests Trevor Francis' £1m move from Birmingham City to Forest in 1979 – English football's first £1m transfer – would equate to about £173m in today's market.

That does not mean the fees paid for Rogers, Anderson and Tonali were modest. Rather, it illustrates how dramatically Premier League revenues have grown, allowing clubs to spend sums that would once have been unimaginable.

When Jack Grealish became Britain's first £100m footballer when joining City from Villa in 2021, the transfer marked another turning point in the evolution of the market.

Only five years later, nine-figure deals are no longer viewed as extraordinary. They have become a familiar feature of Premier League transfer windows.

The next milestone may not be far away.

While no Premier League club has yet paid £200m for a player, football's financial ceiling continues to rise and the first transfer of that magnitude no longer feels beyond the realms of possibility.

Will the World Cup affect Arsenal's transfer plans?

title: Vote for the Register male Athlete of the Week for July 12-18
description: Here are the Register's male Athlete of the Week candidates from the metro area for the week of July 12-18.
fullText:
The week of July 12-18 saw the IHSAA state baseball tournament field set for all four classes.

Eight teams from the Des Moines metro area qualified for the state baseball tournament. Indianola, Waukee, Johnston, West Des Moines Valley and Southeast Polk qualified in Class 4A; Carlisle and North Polk advanced in 3A and Ankeny Christian is competing in 1A.

There were several standout individual performances from area baseball players during substate competition.

Here are the Des Moines Register's male Athlete of the Week candidates from the Des Moines metro area for the week of July 12-18. DesMoinesRegister.com readers vote to determine the winners, who will be announced at the end of the week.

Mincks threw a strong complete game for the No. 6 Johnston baseball team during its 2-1 victory over Dowling Catholic in the 4A Substate 3 championship game on July 15 at Johnston.

Mincks surrendered one run on four hits and one walk in seven innings to earn the win and help his team advance to state. He finished the game with four strikeouts.

Berg had an explosive hitting night for No. 2 Carlisle during its 14-5 victory over Harlan in the 3A Substate 8 championship game on July 15 at Carlisle.

Berg went 3-for-4 with one double and two solo home runs. He also was 3-for-4 with a solo home run during a 9-8 substate semifinal win over Winterset in eight innings on July 13 at Carlisle.

Sanders helped Southeast Polk upset No. 5 Cedar Rapids Kennedy in an 11-inning marathon during the 4A Substate 5 championship game on July 15 at Cedar Rapids.

Sanders doubled once, picked up one sacrifice hit and drove in three runs for the Rams.

Blake was brilliant on the mound for No. 3 Indianola during its 8-0 victory over No. 8 Waukee Northwest in the 4A Substate 8 championship game on July 15 at Indianola.

Blake fired a complete-game two-hit shutout for the Indians. He struck out nine batters and did not issue a walk.

Fincham helped Ankeny Christian upset No. 3 Saint Ansgar in a 12-10 slugfest during the 1A Substate 3 championship game on July 14 at Mason City.

Fincham went 3-for-5 at the plate with a triple. He scored three runs and tallied two steals and RBIs apiece.

Joe Randleman covers high school sports for the Ames Tribune. Contact him at jrandleman@gannett.com. Follow him on Twitter at @JoeRandleman

This article originally appeared on Des Moines Register: Vote for the Register male Athlete of the Week for July 12-18

title: Vote for the Register female Athlete of the Week for July 12-18
description: Here are the Register's female Athlete of the Week candidates from the metro area for the week of July 12-18.
fullText:
The week of July 12-18 saw the IGHSAU state softball tournament field set for all five classes.

The Des Moines metro area saw a total of 11 teams qualify for the state softball tournament.

Defending champion Waukee Northwest, Ankeny Centennial, West Des Moines Valley, Dallas Center-Grimes, Southeast Polk and Dowling Catholic are all competing in Class 5A. The 4A field includes Adel-Desoto-Minburn, Carlisle and North Polk; Two-time defending 2A champion Van Meter is now competing for a 3A state title and Grand View Christian will be playing in 2A.

There were several outstanding individual performances from area softball players during regional final competition last week.

Here are the Des Moines Register's female Athlete of the Week candidates from the Des Moines metro area for the week of July 12-18. DesMoinesRegister.com readers vote to determine the winners, who will be announced at the end of the week.

Fetters had a big night swinging the bat for the 5A No. 4 DCG softball team during its 11-3 victory over No. 15 Cedar Rapids Prairie in the 5A Region 4 championship game on July 14 at Grimes.

Fetters went 2-for-3 with a home run. She both scored and drove in two runs apiece to help the Mustangs advance to state.

Thamontri keyed No. 6 Southeast Polk's 5-0 victory over Ottumwa in the 5A Region 6 championship game on July 14 at Pleasant Hill.

The Ram catcher went a perfect 3-for-3 at the plate with one double. She scored and drove in one run apiece and also added one steal.

Lucas helped the No. 8 North Polk softball team down Boone, 5-1, in the 4A Region 6 championship game on July 14 at Alleman.

Lucas was 1-for-3 at the plate, and she drove in three runs for the Comets. She also made two putouts and had no errors in the field.

Karaidos helped No. 6 Van Meter pound No. 8 Atlantic by a 9-1 score in the 3A Region 5 championship game on July 14 at Van Meter.

The Bulldog third baseman was 3-for-4 with a double and home run. She finished the game with two runs and three RBIs.

Cox helped No. 2 Grand View Christian roll past No. 14 West Marshall by a 12-4 score in the 2A Region 1 champion game on July 13 in Des Moines.

Cox was the winning pitcher after allowing two earned runs on eight hits with six strikeouts and one walk in five innings. She also homered and drove in three runs for the Thunder.

title: Spain Humiliates Trump After He Refuses to Get Out of Victory Photos
description: Donald Trump presented Spain’s World Cup team with the trophy ... and then refused to leave.
fullText:
Spain’s World Cup-winning team has made it abundantly clear that they did not want Donald Trump in their victory photos.

The official account for Spain’s national football league shared an altered image of the country’s celebration in New Jersey Sunday, snipping the U.S. president out of photos after he refused to leave the dais. The FIFA World Cup account also cropped Trump out of the shot, leaving only the president’s shoulder in view.

Trump handed the World Cup trophy to Spain’s team alongside FIFA President Gianni Infantino, after they beat Argentina 1-0. But the U.S. president then refused to leave the picture, apparently trying to run out time before the traditional trophy lift by chatting with the players as he jockeyed for a prime position in front of the group.

Spain’s team, however, was not having it. Video footage of the historic celebration captured several players politely pushing Trump away as they prepared for their official photograph.

Spain’s players refused to celebrate with Trump after the U.S. president attempted to insert himself into the middle of their victory photo. He was politely pushed aside. pic.twitter.com/x82yxxCZgw

— Anonymous (@YourAnonCentral) July 20, 2026

Yet Trump stayed anyway, lingering beside the team even after repeated attempts by Infantino to pull him off the stage. He finally exited only after the team lifted its trophy and blew the confetti cannons in a glaring attempt to ensure he’d be included in the photograph.

His insistence on remaining in frame followed weeks of scathing remarks by Trump against Spain and its prime minister, Pedro Sánchez, over NATO defense spending. Trump has referred to Spain as “hopeless,” derided its people as “bad,” and also threatened to “cut off all trade” with the longtime U.S. ally as he ramped up his NATO disputes. In the months prior to that, he also raged against Spain after they refused to let the U.S. military use Spanish bases to bomb Iran.

None of that hostility was on display after the game, however—at least publicly. Back at Joint Base Andrews, Trump said he had spoken to Spain and “congratulated them on having a great team.” He also noted that he had “no tension” with the country’s leadership.

“I have no tension with anybody,” Trump remarked cavalierly.

title: Uefa president Aleksander Ceferin boycotts World Cup final in protest against Fifa
description: Fifa has come under heavy scrutiny during the 2026 World Cup, with the debacle surrounding Folarin Balogun just one aspect of Uefa’s discontent
fullText:
Uefa president Aleksander Ceferin stayed away from the World Cup final in protest, with the Folarin Balogun affair understood to be just one aspect of his organisation’s discontent with Fifa.

The Times was first to report on Monday that the Slovenian had stayed away, with the relationship between European football’s governing body and the game’s global federation under severe strain.

It’s understood Ceferin chose not to attend the match in New Jersey, with the Uefa president’s unhappiness understood to cover a number of aspects of Fifa’s handling of the World Cup.

Uefa was publicly critical of the decision by Fifa’s disciplinary committee to suspend a one-match ban for United States striker Balogun, calling it “unprecedented, incomprehensible and unjustifiable” and saying it had “crossed a red line”.

The suspension of the ban followed a call from US president Donald Trump to Fifa president Gianni Infantino asking for a “review” of Balogun’s red card, raising questions of political influence in Fifa’s decision-making.

That influence certainly did not appear to be reciprocal, with Fifa powerless to prevent the US authorities sending home Somali referee Omar Artan before the tournament had even started. Uefa subsequently appointed Artan to officiate next month’s Uefa Super Cup final.

Fifa also failed to ensure equitable treatment for Iran compared to the other 47 nations, with the Asian nation subject to strict conditions on their entry to and exit from the US. Their coach, Amir Ghalenoei, said his team were the “most oppressed” at the World Cup, while US Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin said he “danced a happy dance” when the team were eliminated.

There is also discontent at Uefa over Fifa’s apparent willingness to bend or break football’s laws at the tournament – with hydration breaks effectively splitting matches into four quarters and allowing broadcasters to cut to adverts, and the 27-minute half-time interval at the World Cup final well in excess of the maximum 15 minutes permitted.

There are also no plans for Uefa to adopt other changes introduced for the World Cup – such as the interpretation of ‘mistaken identity’ VAR protocol which led to Switzerland’s Breel Embolo being sent off in the quarter-final against Argentina, or the dismissal of players who cover their mouths when talking to an opponent.

Ceferin has also been publicly critical of Fifa’s ticket pricing strategy for the tournament, with Uefa having revealed that 40 per cent of tickets for Euro 2028 will be in the most affordable categories and priced at around £30 or £60, and having also pledged not to adopt dynamic pricing.

Ceferin did attend the opening match of the tournament in Mexico City on 11 June.

title: Lamine Yamal celebrates World Cup win with little brother who charmed fans
description: The three-year-old brother of Yamal has been hailed one of Spain's World Cup breakout stars by fans
fullText:
Spanish footballer Lamine Yamal may have lifted the World Cup but it is his little brother Kenye who appears to have stolen the spotlight.

The three-year-old quickly become an internet sensation after footage of his exuberant celebrations following Spain's 1-0 victory over Argentina spread across social media on Sunday.

While the footballer, just 19 himself, continued his rise by helping Spain achieve football's biggest prize, attention swiftly turned to his younger brother's joyful reactions from the sidelines.

From sticking out his tongue on the big screen to wildly cheering on his older brother, Kenye has been hailed by fans as one of the unexpected breakout stars of the 2026 tournament.

Spain triumphed in a battle of endurance, beating Argentina with a goal in the 106th minute at the New York New Jersey Stadium.

The team's second World Cup win has led to Lamine Yamal becoming the youngest player ever to win both the European Championship and the World Cup.

His younger brother Kenye has become a regular fixture both on and off the pitch - often featuring in Lamine Yamal's TikToks and charming fans from around the world.

The three-year-old, who appears keen to follow in his big brother's footsteps, is the son of Lamine Yamal's mother Sheila Ebana.

Speaking about Kenye, Lamine Yamal previously told outlets: "I'm moved when I see my little brother this happy, as well as seeing my mum and friends living the life they always dreamed of.

"My little brother means everything to me. I am in love with him; it feels like he is my son."

In what could be seen as a symbolic passing of the torch, Lamine Yamal went head to head with Argentinan football legend Lionel Messi for the first time during the World Cup final.

The two had crossed paths years earlier in 2007 when Messi, then 20, was seen cradling and bathing a tiny baby boy - Lamine Yamal - after his mother won a competition to meet a Barcelona first-team player.

Days ahead of Sunday's final, Messi, now 39, described Lamine Yamal as "one of the best" footballers right now.

"He's a tremendous player - a global star. He's 19 years old and has his whole career ahead of him. I wish him the best, but we'll give it our all to ensure he doesn't become champion this time," he added.

As the full-time whistle blew, cementing Spain's victory, the pair could be seen embracing on the pitch.

title: 55 days till Arizona Cardinals' opener: Remembering the 1955 season
description: With 55 days until the Arizona Cardinals' 2026 opener, let's look back at their 1955 season.
fullText:
We continue the countdown to the start of the Arizona Cardinals' regular season. Today, Monday, July 20, is 55 days away from the Arizona Cardinals' regular-season opener on Sept. 13 on the road against the Los Angeles Chargers.

With 55 days until Week 1, let's look back at the 1955 season for the Cardinals.

The Cardinals went 4-7-1 im 1955. They started 2-1 but went 1-4-1 over their next 6 games. They were ninth in the league in scoring (out of 12 teams), averaging 18.7 points per game, and gave up 21.0 per game, which was eighth in the league.

They were held to 7 or fewer points four times and shut out twice.

The Cardinals had four Pro Bowlers.

Matson was also a first-team All-Pro.

Lamar HcHan started 11 games at quarterback and led the team with 1,085 passing yards, 11 touchdown passes and 19 interceptions.

Matson led the team with 475 rushing yards. He caught 17 passes for 239 yards and returned two punts for touchdowns.

Don Stonesifer was their leading receiver with 28 catches for 330 yards and five touchdowns.

Lane and safety Tom Keane each had six interceptions on defense.

Get more Cardinals and NFL coverage from Cards Wire's Jess Root and others by listening to the latest on the Rise Up, See Red podcast. Subscribe on Spotify, YouTube or Apple Podcasts.
