title: Donald Trump wants World Cup back in U.S. 'immediately'
description: Donald Trump wants the World Cup in the U.S. again while still in office, but he will have to wait awhile for a return.
fullText:
The United States just finished hosting the 2026 World Cup for the first time in more than 30 years, and even though it will be a while before it does it again, President Donald Trump will be requesting to host it once more "immediately."

Trump made the comments to Fox Sports' Jenny Taft ahead of the World Cup final between Spain and Argentina on Sunday, July 19, lauding the success of the tournament that saw millions of people flock to the U.S., Canada and Mexico, and take part in a widely successful event.

He admitted to FIFA president Gianni Infantino that he was skeptical of how well the World Cup would do in the U.S., but the great turnout and reception resulted in him wanting it once more.

"I had a great idea for Gianni. I said, ‘You have to do two countries.’ So announce us again next time, and announce another country after that, that’ll take out some of the anger and the shock," Trump said. "But based on the numbers, we will be requesting it again immediately. But I said to take out the shock, you could announce us and maybe then for the next one another, and even another. But we have to do this again, and we have to do it while I’m around. You hear that, Gianni?”

If Trump was referring to the World Cup being in the U.S. again while he is in office, it won't happen since his term ends in 2028. The 2030 World Cup will be hosted by Morocco, Spain and Portugal, while the 2034 edition will be in Saudi Arabia.

The next available World Cup is in 2038, which the U.S. could very likely host since Oceania and North America will be eligible to host under the current cycle rules. However, that's in 12 years.

Trump has made his presence within FIFA known since 2018, when the U.S. was awarded the hosting duties for the 2026 tournament. He has become a close friend with Infantino, who presented Trump with the FIFA Peace Prize in December in a controversial move. He also inserted himself into the red card conversation surrounding U.S. star Folarin Balogun, when he pushed Infantino to reverse the decision to suspend him for one match, which ended up happening in another decision surrounded by controversy.

The president was inside MetLife Stadium for the final between Spain and Argentina, and was on the field to present the medals and trophy for Spain's title, which included him awkwardly still on stage as La Roja celebrated holding the trophy.

This article originally appeared on USA TODAY: Donald Trump wants World Cup back in U.S. 'immediately'

title: From Super Bowl to World Cup: The major sporting events Trump has attended in his 2nd term
description: President Donald Trump has prioritized attending huge sporting events during his second term.
fullText:
President Donald Trump has prioritized attending huge sporting events during his second term.

On Sunday, Trump will become the first U.S. president to attend a men’s World Cup final when Argentina and Spain face off in one of the year’s most anticipated sporting events. His appearance marks just the latest sporting event he will attend since returning to office in January 2025.

Here’s the list of other major sporting events he has attended since returning to office.

Trump became the first sitting U.S. president to attend a Super Bowl during on Feb. 9, 2025 in New Orleans. The game came about a month after a New Year’s Day terrorist attack in the city killed 14 people and injured dozens more. Trump met with families of the victims before the game.

Trump left the game about 10 minutes into the second half—which left pundits speculating that he left early because his desired team, the Kansas City Chiefs, was losing.

Trump, who is close with UFC President Dana White, has attended multiple fights during his second term in office. He was met with cheers when he attended a fight at the Kaseya Center in Miami alongside Secretary of State Marco Rubio in April 2025. He later attended a UFC match with Rubio at the Prudential Center in Newark, New Jersey in June 2025.

Trump reaffirmed his love for the UFC after he recently hosted a fight on the South Lawn of the White House on June 14 to celebrate America’s 250th anniversary.

Trump attended the 2025 Daytona 500 just weeks after going to Super Bowl LIX. He had also attended the popular race in 2020 during his first term in office. His appearance was marked by his motorcade taking a lap around the Daytona International Speedway.

Trump’s appearance at the World Cup final on Sunday is not the first time he attended a FIFA event. Last year, Trump was mocked after he awkwardly joined in on Chelsea FC’s FIFA Club World Cup celebration.

Chelsea winger Cole Palmer later said he did not realize Trump would be on the stage as his team lifted up their trophy.

“I knew he was going to be there, but I didn’t know he was going [to] be on the stand where we lift the trophy. So, I was a bit confused,” said Palmer, via USA TODAY.

Trump faced widespread criticism after his attendance at the U.S. Open men’s tennis final snarled security lines, which delayed fans getting into the USTA Billie Jean King National Tennis Center in September 2025.

The 2025 match between world No. 1 Jannik Sinner and No. 2 Carlos Alcaraz started about 30 minutes late as a result of enhanced security measures. Trump also faced backlash after it was reported that US Open officials sent an email to broadcasters asking them to censor any negative reactions to his presence.

Trump made waves after fans blamed the president for “cursing” the New York Knicks with his attendance at Game 3 of the NBA Finals at Madison Square Garden.

The NYPD and the Secret Service cancelled the popular watch party outside the world’s most famous arena, leaving die-hard fans outraged. Fans were also prohibited from bringing bags inside the arena, and were told to expect enhanced security measures before entering the game.

Trump was also met with boos as he was shown on screen before tip-off.

The Knicks ended up breaking their 13-game winning streak in Game 3, making it their only loss in the best-of-seven matchup with the San Antonio Spurs.

title: Leandro Paredes sent off after final whistle as Argentina’s World Cup ends with punches and in disgrace
description: Paredes was involved in an ugly clash with Spain’s Eric Garcia and Gavi following the full-time whistle
fullText:
Argentina’s World Cup ended with punches thrown and the former champions left in disgrace following a 1-0 defeat to Spain in the final.

There were chaotic scenes after the full-time whistle in New Jersey as the Spain substitutes ran onto the pitch to celebrate victory.

Nahuel Molina, the Argentina defender, appeared to swing a punch into the stomach of Spain captain Rodri as he ran past him onto the field.

Then, there was a violent clash involving midfielder Leandro Paredes, who was pictured shoving Spain’s Eric Garcia by the throat.

Paredes wrestled Spain’s Gavi to the ground and pushed into his face while holding him by his substitute bib.

Spain’s Mikel Oyarzabal and Rodri sprinted past Molina after the final whistle was blown and La Roja were crowned champions.

Molina appeared to swing at Rodri, leading to the Spain captain stopping in his tracks and approaching the Argentina defender while shouting.

Molina then squared up to Rodri and bumped him in the chest, which was the flashpoint that triggered Spain’s Eric Garcia to step in.

Paredes, who was booked during the final and was fortunate to not receive a second yellow card, then flew towards Garcia. He jabbed him in the throat just as Argentina coach Lionel Scaloni approached, wagging his finger and trying to break up the scuffles.

With Scaloni separating Garcia and other Argentina coaching staff members getting involved, Paredes then turned his attention to Spain’s 21-year-old midfielder Gavi - who by that point was already wrestling with Argentina’s Thiago Almada.

Paredes, 32, spun Gavi to the ground and appeared to push into his face while holding him by the bib. Several of Gavi’s Spanish team-mates then stepped in to shield him, with Spain’s Borja Iglesias crashing into Paredes.

It was a sorry way for Argentina’s tournament to end after they had taken an emotional path to the final. They were attempting to a second World Cup in a row for captain Lionel Messi, who was playing on the World Cup stage for the final time.

But the antics of the rest of the Argentina team were quickly condemned as the scenes after the final whistle soured Spain’s deserved victory, with former England goalkeeper Joe Hart labelling Argentina’s behaviour “disgusting”.

"It's a disgrace,” Gary Neville said on ITV. “I love the competitiveness of the Argentines, fighting and scrapping for their lives, Messi has propped them up, but it’s been a disgrace the last few games.”

“Paredes threw two or three haymakers into someone's face. He went for them,” Alan Shearer said on BBC. “There is no place or space for that.

“We know how much it means to them and we know how much it hurts to lose, but there is a way to lose. Too many times we have seen that from Argentina. The reaction after the final whistle is terrible.”

BBC pundit Hart said: “There was one man on the pitch who did have class and that was Lionel Messi, who shook hands with every one of those Spanish players.“The game’s done. They could have absolutely no complaints about what happened today. Disgusting behaviour.”

Hart added: “Spain fully deserve it. They have been absolutely superb all tournament.“They have dominated all game against a rigid, rugged Argentina. The best team has won this World Cup.”

Argentina were previously criticised for their aggressive behaviour during their 2-1 semi-final win over England, after which players unveiled a banner claiming sovereignty of the Falklands Islands.

Fellow pundit Wayne Rooney said of Argentina’s behaviour: “Does it surprise you? It doesn’t surprise me.“We’ve seen this with Argentina before. It’s just not the reaction you want. We’ve seen some of the reaction after knocking England out in the last round.“That’s just sad. If you lose a game of football, be graceful and get out of there. It’s really poor from them.”

ITV’s co-commentators were in agreement that Spain were deserved winners, with Lee Dixon accusing Argentina of “anti-football” and questioning how Paredes had remained on the pitch, while Ally McCoist said Spain were “the only team trying to win this game”.

Meanwhile, the tears rolled down Lionel Messi’s face as he watched the Argentina fans following defeat to Spain.

Messi, 39, is yet to confirm his future plans but the expectation is that one of the greatest to grace the game has played his final World Cup before retirement.

The Argentina captain was left frustrated as Spain dominated the final, with the European champions winning 1-0 thanks to Ferran Torres’ goal after extra time.

Messi had inspired a series of brilliant comebacks in the knockout stages to help Argentina to a second successive final but Spain were deserved winners.

Messi scored eight goals in an incredible tournament and the Argentina fans chanted his name as Spain’s Rodri collected the player of tournament award.

Messi then led his team-mates to collect their runners-up medals. He then stood and cried while he watched the Argentina fans serenade him.

The record eight-time Ballon d’Or winner will be 43 at the next men’s World Cup, which takes place in Spain, Portugal and Morocco - but will kick off in Argentina.

Messi waited until Spain lifted the World Cup, but then quickly left the pitch in what was perhaps a bitter end to his World Cup journey.

Elsewhere, Argentina centre-back Cristian Romero snubbed Donald Trump’s handshake during the presentation of the runners-up medals.

Romero started the match at the heart of the Argentine defence, alongside Lisandro Martinez, but was subbed off at the 70-minute mark.

However, the 28-year-old Tottenham defender still made his mark after that as he snubbed a handshake from Trump when the US president was conducting the medal presentation.

Trump was handing out medals to both teams, as well the individual tournament awards, and Romero shook the hand of both Fifa president Gianni Infantino and Mexican president Claudia Sheinbaum, who were either side of Trump, but did not engage with the US president.

title: Jaguars' Hunter Makes Defensive '2026 NFL All-Breakout Team'
description: After a rookie season lost to injury, the two-way superstar shifts focus to cornerback, where his elite ball-tracking skills could transform Jacksonville’s defense into a legitimate Super Bowl contender.
fullText:
Big things are expected for Jacksonville Jaguars' two-way superstar Travis Hunter in Year 2.

After his rookie season was cut short after only seven games because of a knee injury, Hunter is finally set to be fully unleashed on the rest of the NFL in 2026.

Hunter was primarily used at wide receiver as a rookie, but is expected to become a full-time cornerback this year, only occasionally being used on offense. Hunter is believed to have All-Pro-level ability at corner, and Jacksonville's entire defense should benefit from having him on the field.

NFL.com analyst Bucky Brooks believes Hunter will excel in his new full-time role and has named Hunter to his defensive 2026 NFL All-Breakout Team.

It may be a season later than expected, but Brooks is expecting a big year from Hunter in 2026. Cornerback may be Hunter's best position in the NFL, despite his talents at WR, and Brooks believes he can become an outstanding defender in 2026.

"The No. 2 overall pick in the 2025 draft was expected to revolutionize the modern NFL as a two-way player, but his debut campaign came to an abrupt end with a knee injury in October. In Year 2, the Jaguars are expected to utilize Hunter as a full-time cornerback and part-time offensive playmaker, giving him a major role for a Jacksonville team that just went 13-4. As a natural ballhawk with outstanding instincts, vision and ball skills, Hunter's presence on the island for a turnover-obsessed unit could be the difference between another early exit from the playoffs and a deep postseason run."

Brooks believes that Hunter's presence on defense can put the Jaguars over the top in 2026. That is exactly the kind of player Jacksonville was counting on when they traded up to draft the Heisman Trophy winner second overall.

Despite the objections from Deion Sanders, Hunter's coach at Colorado, the Jaguars may be making the right decision to have the 23-year-old focus on defense this season. If Hunter can become a true shutdown corner, the Jags' defense could be top-5 in the entire NFL.

With Trevor Lawrence leading what looks like a potent offense, even with Hunter only contributing occasionally, Jacksonville has a great chance to not only make the playoffs again, but to do some damage once they get there.

The debate over whether Hunter should play wide receiver, cornerback, or both is probably going to rage on for the entirety of his career.

However, if Hunter can become an All-Pro at CB, which he certainly has the ability to, it will be hard to argue that Jacksonville made the wrong decision by playing him on defense full-time.

The Jaguars need Hunter on defense more than they need him on offense in 2026, making it an easy choice. Brooks believes that decision will pay off big-time for Jacksonville, and Hunter will prove them right by having a breakout season.

The thought of Hunter playing cornerback should be terrifying to opposing offenses and quarterbacks this season.

title: LA sports figure Jeff Pearlman admits Angels have helped him ‘rediscover his love for baseball’
description: It’s been a long and exhausting season for the Angels, but it appears the team has helped at least one fan rediscover his love for baseball. New York Times award-winning...
fullText:
It’s been a long and exhausting season for the Angels, but it appears the team has helped at least one fan rediscover his love for baseball.

New York Times award-winning sports author Jeff Pearlman released a video on TikTok, revealing the Angels have rekindled his passion for the sport.

“In the last [month] I’ve gone to probably like five games, three of the five just by myself, and you may think, ‘What kind of loser goes to a baseball game by himself?’ It’s [the] best,” Pearlman said during the TikTok video. “I buy a ticket really high up. … I get free parking because I park offsite, and I just love it.”

“I love the feel of the ballpark, I love the look of the ballpark, I love the green, I love the national anthem, the seventh-inning stretch, I love that the Angels always do these really cheesy giveaways.”

Despite the Angels having a poor record (39-61), Pearlman said he enjoys being able to watch baseball at an affordable price and called it the “cheapest, best bang for your buck ever.”

Pearlman went on to compare how cheap the Angels tickets are compared with Dodgers tickets. He said he’s happy not having to spend $40 to park and spending over $100 for a ticket.

Pearlman is a New York Times best-selling author who writes about football, baseball and basketball. He also wrote a biography on Tupac Shakur.

Some notable books Pearlman has written about LA sports in his career include “Showtime: Magic, Kareem, Riley, and the Los Angeles Lakers Dynasty of the 1980s,” and “Three-Ring Circus: Kobe, Shaq, Phil, and the Crazy Years of the Lakers Dynasty.”

Now attending Angels games for the experience to watch baseball in its purest form, Pearlman said he’s been taking photos with fans in attendance and leaving free copies of his books across the stadium. He enjoys getting a cold brew coffee from the Rise Espresso Bar in the stadium that includes cold foam and Cocoa Puffs.

The 2026 season has been another tough one for Angels fans, and the franchise might lose 100 games in a season for the first time.

Fans at Angel Stadium have been voicing their frustrations toward ownership this year. Earlier this season, they organized protests toward owner Arte Moreno, urging him to sell the team due to another losing season.

The protests have also been happening inside the stadium as fans have been participating in the Tarps Off movement, which has included regular chants of “Sell the team,” echoing from the right field seats.

Pearlman admitted he has mixed feelings about Tarps Off but called it “entertaining.”

“My new happy place is going to Angels games, parking for free, knowing I have a $5 ticket, watching the game, presuming the Angels are probably going to lose,” Pearlman said. “Just relishing in the moment.”

title: Is Trump the first president to attend a World Cup final?
description: President Donald Trump will be attending the final men’s World Cup game in East Rutherford, New Jersey on Sunday afternoon.
fullText:
President Donald Trump will be attending the final men’s World Cup game in East Rutherford, New Jersey on Sunday afternoon.

Argentina and Spain are set to face off for the World Cup final at 3 p.m. ET in East Rutherford, New Jersey after a month of thrilling games that have gripped sports fans across the globe. The White House confirmed last week that Trump will be attending Sunday’s highly anticipated game as the U.S. caps off hosting one of the largest sporting events in the world.

“His attendance will cap what has been the most watched, most secure, and most successful World Cup in American history,” White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt told reporters. “This is a fitting conclusion to a tournament that showcased America’s ability to host the world on the grandest stage.”

FIFA president Gianni Infantino, a close ally of the president, also confirmed last month on “Fox & Friends” that he and Trump will jointly present the trophy to the winners of the World Cup.

“We will be together with the president enjoying the final and handing the trophy to the winner, of course, together,” Infantino said.

Trump faced criticism earlier this month after it was reported that he called Infantino asking FIFA to overturn the suspension of U.S. men’s national team striker Folarin Balogun. Balogun was ultimately allowed to play, but the U.S. men’s national team still lost to Belgium.

The appearance will mark the first time a U.S. president will attend the men’s World Cup final. The U.S. has hosted the tournament only once before, under then-President Bill Clinton.

When the U.S. last hosted the men’s World Cup in 1994, Clinton delivered remarks at the opening ceremony but did not attend the final match between Brazil and Italy. Instead, then-Vice President Al Gore attended the final game in Pasadena, California and handed the trophy to Brazil’s team captain.

But Clinton did attend the women’s final game in Pasadena when the U.S. women’s soccer team clinched their second World Cup title in 1999.

Trump makes prediction on World Cup winner: ‘It’s hard to bet against Messi’

From Super Bowl to World Cup: The major sporting events Trump has attended in his 2nd term

title: Is Kyle Dubas Running Out of Time?
description: Sidney Crosby is in his last year of his current contract and potentially career. Is Kyle Dubas doing enough to give him a legitimate run at a Stanely Cup? The Penguins have been fairly quiet this offseason. They acquired Nicholas Robertson from the Toronto Maple Leafs and signed him to a two-year deal worth $3.25…
fullText:
Sidney Crosby is in his last year of his current contract and potentially career. Is Kyle Dubas doing enough to give him a legitimate run at a Stanely Cup?

The Penguins have been fairly quiet this offseason. They acquired Nicholas Robertson from the Toronto Maple Leafs and signed him to a two-year deal worth $3.25 million AAV. They also signed Andrei Kuzmenko to a one-year, $5 million AAV contract. After that, they proceeded to lose 30+ goal scorer Anthony Mantha to division rival New Jersey on a deal carrying a $4.75 million cap hit.

Robertson scored 16 goals last season, while Kuzmenko tallied just 13 in only 52 games after suffering a knee injury in February that sidelined him until mid-April.

Kuzmenko’s production projects to approximately 21 goals over a full 84-game season, meaning he and Robertson would combine for roughly 37 goals to replace Mantha’s 33. The question is: will that be enough to give Sidney Crosby one last legitimate run?

With the majority of the free agents now signed, here are the top three options still available:

Laine played only five games last season before suffering a core injury that required surgery in October 2025. He was cleared to return in January but was a healthy scratch for the Canadiens after being medically cleared.

That alone tells you quite a bit about where things stand with Laine. He hasn’t scored 30 goals since his third season in 2018-19. While he did score 20 goals two seasons ago, staying healthy remains a major concern and creates significant uncertainty about what he can provide on the ice.

Kevin Hayes was already with the Penguins last season, but he appeared in just 28 games because of an upper-body injury sustained during training camp, followed by another unrelated upper-body injury in March 2026.

He has recorded just 31 total points over his last two combined seasons. If Hayes were truly the solution, I think he would have already been re-signed. His declining offensive production doesn’t give me much confidence that he can help extend this final run.

Evander Kane is the best option of the three in my opinion, although this move wouldn’t come without risk.

When healthy, he has shown he can still contribute offensively and provide valuable secondary scoring. However, he has also developed a reputation from multiple reports as being difficult in the locker room. While off-ice issues shouldn’t always determine a roster decision, when they’re hockey-related they have to factor into the equation. I also think it says something that a struggling Vancouver team appears unwilling to re-sign the hometown player.

If anyone is worth taking a chance on, though, it’s Kane. You take the good with the bad and hope that, surrounded by a better team and staying healthy, he can produce somewhere in the 20-25 goal range.

This is likely Crosby’s last run unless he decides to sign another extension after the season. With Evgeni Malkin also expected to be finished after next year and more organizational changes likely on the horizon, those factors could influence Crosby to finish his career elsewhere if he wants to continue playing.

There is also the wildcard possibility that the Penguins attempt to acquire Jason Robertson from Dallas. Now that they’ve acquired his brother, it could become a factor in Jason wanting to sign an extension after arbitration, regardless of the tax situation.

Obviously, that deal would require a significant package, and I’m not even sure the cap hit would fit. However, moving out salary could create enough room to bring in Robertson on what would likely be a cost-controlled short-term contract following arbitration. If Kyle Dubas wants to be aggressive, it could pay huge dividends, but it would also carry substantial risk.

I really think, based on Dubas’ personality and the direction of his process, he’s running out of time. In particular, not re-signing Anthony Mantha for $4.75 million feels like a missed opportunity, especially considering the amount of cap space Pittsburgh still has.

A Jason Robertson trade feels like a Cinderella scenario, but if it somehow happened, it would completely change the outlook of this team. It’s an interesting position for Dubas to be in right now, and the Penguins are definitely a team to watch.

title: 'The last of Messi': Argentines mourn loss and end of an era
description: Nervous excitement and rowdy cheers turned into anguished groans, nailbiting tension and tears, as Argentines' hope of yet another epic comeback led by Lionel Messi in the World Cup fizzled out against Spain.Empty because of Messi, because I won't see him in a World Cup again.
fullText:
Nervous excitement and rowdy cheers turned into anguished groans, nailbiting tension and tears, as Argentines' hope of yet another epic comeback led by Lionel Messi in the World Cup fizzled out against Spain.

At the Cafe Relax bar in the historic center of Buenos Aires, fans with tears in their eyes after the final whistle blew on the 1-0 defeat said that what saddened them most was that it was almost certainly 39-year-old Messi's last shot at a World Cup.

"I feel empty. Empty because of Messi, because I won't see him in a World Cup again. This is the last one," said Camilo Cichero, a 22-year-old architecture student who was comforting his crying friend.

"It's not how we expected it to go. At least it gives me peace of mind knowing he's already won everything there was to win."

Composing himself, his friend Juan Saenz, 22, a law student said: "This was the last of Messi ...it was the very end, and it finished this way."

"I was born while Messi was playing, and this was the end of that era."

Around the city fireworks went off, car horns honked, and thousands of fans still made their way to the iconic Obelisk, the traditional place for football celebrations.

Initially crestfallen, they soon began partying again to celebrate their team and many said that despite the defeat, they would take to the streets to welcome the team home on Monday.

"Thank you so much, players!!! You fought until the very end. Argentina will always stand tall," President Javier Milei wrote on X.

After a scrappy World Cup run built on a series of narrow victories, Argentines were confident they could pull off a second victory in a row in the final in New Jersey.

Draped in their blue-and-white flag and donning the national football jersey, they braved an icy cold day to pack bars and public squares, fizzing with excitement at the start of the game.

In Cafe Relax, which displays a flag saying it is "forbidden to forget" the Falkland Islands, which Argentina invaded in 1982 only to be ejected by a British task force, nervous energy and excited chants gave way to tension by half-time.

In the second half angry shouts at the screen were interspersed with excited chants, and increasingly foul language, while some sat arms folded, feet tapping and chewing their nails or burying their heads in the flag.

"Oh no, it can't be, please, please!" wailed one woman when Enzo Fernandez was sent off for two yellow cards.

Before extra time began, Teresa Dolores del Rio Varela, 87, complained that "it drags on and on, and you start to get nervous. I'm happy, but I just want it to end because it's getting long."

Joaquin Romero, 22, who works in sales said the game was "incredibly complicated. I don't know how it'll happen, but it's going to happen for us. We're Argentine. We're champions and we're going to win."

A dramatic extra-time goal by Ferran Torres cast a pall of silence over the bar for the last agonizing minutes.

Cichero said he was sad for Messi but dismayed at the game.

"I didn't like how Argentina played. I didn't like the game plan, the substitutions, the players. I didn't like the referee."

Abel Torres, a 62-year-old retiree wearing a blue-and-white jester hat and blowing a vuvuzela throughout the game, teared up as he described the "incredibly emotional" game.

"Argentina gave it their all. Today was a true example of what Argentina is all about: that despite the hard times and the obstacles we face, we always rise again."

In Messi's hometown Rosario, hundreds gathered to celebrate the player and his team with fireworks and chants.

"We've already won a cup. We can't just break down in tears -- we have to celebrate. Messi has already put us at the very top of the world," Sergio Irrazabal told AFP.

title: Wilkes-Barre/Scranton Penguins celebrate Christmas in July
description: Jul. 19—WILKES-BARRE — Baby, it was warm outside on Sunday, but the Wilkes-Barre/Scranton Penguins were getting into the holiday spirit with a Christmas in July community event on the Toyota Sportsplex Dek Hockey Rink. The event featuring vendors, skating, and other family-friendly activities, was held in benefit of the Penguins Goals Foundation, which supports and creates programs to make ...
fullText:
Jul. 19—WILKES-BARRE — Baby, it was warm outside on Sunday, but the Wilkes-Barre/Scranton Penguins were getting into the holiday spirit with a Christmas in July community event on the Toyota Sportsplex Dek Hockey Rink.

The event featuring vendors, skating, and other family-friendly activities, was held in benefit of the Penguins Goals Foundation, which supports and creates programs to make youth sports more accessible.

Admission was free, while proceeds from raffles went toward the organization.

Attendees enjoyed an extended open skate, holiday crafts, cookie decorating, and Christmas music. Pictures with Santa and Mrs. Claus in their finest summer attire were also available.

"I love Christmas, so it's a way to get into the holiday spirit and utilize the deck," said Amanda Cromer, community relations and client services manager.

This was the first year of the Christmas in July event, and Cromer was optimistic it would become an annual tradition.

"It was a great turnout. We had a lot of support from people wanting to participate," she said.

Keira Feeney and her son, Jacob, 8, both of Moscow, traveled about half an hour to skate and check out the event.

"We come down here twice a week for Dek hockey," she explained.

The event gave them a family-friendly way to spend the afternoon, and both Feeney and Jacob got into the holiday spirit by wearing Christmas-themed T-shirts.

When it came to actually getting ready for the Christmas season, though, it might have been a bit too early for Jacob.

"I'm ready for the summer to keep going," he said.

For Feeney, although she was not quite ready to start thinking about Christmas, being surrounded by so many vendors forced her to consider ideas for presents.

"I like a lot of the handmade things," she said. "My mom especially, she loves handmade, meaningful things, and fairs like this kinda do that for me."

title: Dana White: Kamaru Usman has 'still got it' despite UFC OKC loss
description: Despite being 1-4 in his past five, Dana White doesn't think Kamaru Usman looks like a fighter who needs to retire after UFC OKC.
fullText:
Dana White doesn't think Kamaru Usman looked like a fighter who needs to retire despite the UFC Fight Night 281 main event not going his way.

Former longtime welterweight champion Usman (21-5 MMA, 16-4 UFC) tried his luck again at middleweight Saturday when he took on former divisional titleholder Dricus Du Plessis (24-3 MMA, 10-1 UFC) at Paycom Center in Oklahoma City. He was outworked for a unanimous decision loss, but had a few shining moments over the 25 minutes.

It was a clear defeat, and Usman is now 1-4 in his past five fights after going on a historic 15-fight winning streak to begin his UFC tenure. At 39, questions about his future are bubbling to the surface, and although UFC CEO White prefers to leave the ultimate decision to the athlete and their team, he saw encouraging signs from Usman in defeat.

"We've seen the, 'Holy shit, these guys should retire' fights. Those weren't it." White told reporters post-fight at UFC Fight Night 281. "I don't know. Obviously a great fight. Fighting a much bigger guy. He's got a great chin – he's still got it. It's not like, 'Oh, Usman's done and he should retire.' He had a great fight tonight. I don't know. He's got to go home, rest, heal up and figure out what's next."

If Usman continues to compete, as he hinted in the aftermath of UFC Fight Night 281 that he would, then "The Nigerian Nightmare" has a choice to make about what weight class he'll compete at.

White said the door is open for Usman to fight at 170 or 185 pounds, and he's earned the respect to pick what he wants without input from the company.

"He's accomplished so much and he's fighting literally the No. 1 or No. 2 best guy in the world, who is way bigger than he is," White said. "I think Kamaru's done enough in his career where it's up to him. These are questions you ask him tonight about how he feels at that weight and what he wants to do. It's not like he's some young guy who hasn't done anything. This guy has done it all."

This article originally appeared on MMA Junkie: Dana White: Kamaru Usman has 'still got it' despite UFC OKC loss

title: Kevin McGoingle and Ty Cobb Have Something in Common
description: The Tigers’ 21-year-old phenom joined baseball royalty by reaching 100 hits in record time, proving his elite hit tool translates seamlessly from top prospect to major league star.
fullText:
Kevin McGonigle has spent his rookie season making unusual numbers feel routine. The latest accomplishment puts him next to Ty Cobb, but the exact wording is worth laying out because there are two separate 100-hit notes connected to the Tigers’ 21-year-old infielder.

McGonigle’s leadoff double against the Los Angeles Angels was the 100th hit of his major league career. He reached the mark in his 95th game. According to the Tigers and Elias Sports Bureau, McGonigle joined Cobb as the only players in franchise history age 21 or younger to collect their first 100 career hits in fewer than 100 games.

Cobb needed 90 games. McGonigle needed 95.

McGonigle worked an eight-pitch plate appearance before hooking the double into right-center field. He later said he did not know about the Cobb connection until after the game and described the comparison as something he had never imagined.

That is the historical note, but it also provides another way to measure how quickly McGonigle’s hitting ability has translated to the majors.

Following Sunday’s game against the Angels, McGonigle had 102 hits through 96 games. He was batting .282 with a .390 on-base percentage and an .815 OPS. His overall line also included 61 runs, 19 doubles, three triples, nine home runs and 11 stolen bases. He ranked sixth in the American League in hits and third in on-base percentage.

The pace started immediately. McGonigle collected four hits, including two doubles, in his major league debut against the San Diego Padres on March 26. He became the second Tigers player and the 21st major leaguer since 1900 to record four hits in his debut.

McGonigle entered the season carrying a 70-grade hit tool from MLB Pipeline and was viewed as the best pure hitter on its preseason Top 100 prospects list. Those evaluations were based on his bat-to-ball ability, strike-zone judgment and willingness to use the entire field. The question was how quickly those traits would work against major league pitching. The first 96 games have provided a fairly direct answer.

There is another layer to the 100-hit mark involving Riley Greene.

Greene and McGonigle became the first two Tigers to reach 100 hits this season, and both recorded their 100th hits on doubles. According to the Tigers’ game notes, they are the first Detroit teammates to each have at least 100 hits through the team’s first 98 games since Miguel Cabrera, Ian Kinsler and Nick Castellanos did it in 2016.

The last pair of left-handed-hitting Tigers teammates to reach 100 hits within the team’s first 98 games were Kirk Gibson and Lou Whitaker in 1985.

That part of the statistic gives the current season some useful context. Greene is in his fifth major league season and has developed into an established part of Detroit’s lineup. McGonigle arrived this spring without a major league plate appearance and reached the same benchmark before the end of July.

There is also a separate age-based season statistic. McGonigle joined Cobb and Al Kaline as the only Tigers age 21 or younger to record at least 100 hits during the team’s first 100 games of a season. Cobb did it in 1907 and 1908, while Kaline accomplished it in 1955 and 1956.

I mean, we are witnessing history of a rookie year unlike we have seen as Tigers fans. The comparison says more about his current pace than where his career will eventually finish.

Cobb reached 100 career hits in 1906, before his first American League batting championship in 1907. He had debuted as an 18-year-old in 1905, batting .240 over 41 games, before improving to .316 in 98 games the following season.

McGonigle has arrived in a different era, with deeper bullpens, specialized relievers and more velocity. He has still accumulated hits without depending on one type of offensive production. The home-run total is growing, but much of his value continues to come from reaching base, producing line drives and extending plate appearances.

Reaching 100 hits in 95 games may not tell us what he is going to be in 10 years but if he is already breaking records that have been dormant for years, maybe it is. We are witnessing something special. McGonigle is that special rookie that we have seen other teams have had around us like Juan Soto in Washington a few seasons back.

Follow me on "X" @rogcastbaseball

title: Spain and UNICEF stole the show: Winners, losers of World Cup final
description: Spain won its second men's World Cup when it beat Argentina in extra time in the final. Here are the winners and losers of the full day.
fullText:
After 104 matches across 16 cities in three countries, Spain reigns supreme in the 2026 World Cup.

La Roja won the tournament by beating Argentina 1-0 in the final on Sunday, July 19 at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, N.J. This is their first title since 2010.

Lionel Messi and Argentina were neutralized and did not have a shot on goal in the entire match, which went into extra time.

After both teams won their groups and then clawed their way through the knockout rounds, a charity photo from 2007 of a young Messi giving a baby Lamine Yamal a bath went viral as a wink of destiny.

Here are the winners and losers of the World Cup final between Spain and Argentina:

If you would have told football fans that Spain was going to win the World Cup after their scoreless opening draw with newcomer Cape Verde, they would have laughed. But La Roja gutted out the tournament as one of the most well-rounded sides playing. Besides winning their second men's World Cup, which is their first since 2010, La Roja nearly ran the table with the player awards, too. AND they are the first country to hold both the men's and women's World Cup titles at the same ... darn ... time.

The Argentina goalkeeper had his work cut out for him as Spain dominated in possession. He broke the World Cup final record with 11 saves in the match, including diving to swat away Lamine Yamal's free kick in second half stoppage time. Even though Argentina lost, Martínez can hold his head high.

Cape Verde is truly the people's champion of the 2026 World Cup. The African nation made its debut in the tournament this summer and rattled Spain in the opening match. They then had Argentina's back against the ropes in the round of 32. Knowing that they gave tough fights to the eventual runner-up and champion, the Blue Sharks have to feel good.

That photo of a young Lionel Messi bathing a baby Lamine Yamal was absolutely EVERYWHERE leading up to the World Cup final. It is actually from a charity photo shoot that UNICEF put together back in 2007. The non-profit was already somewhat of a household name, but this embeds them deep into sports history in a way that no publicist could ever plan.

Argentina's late surge did not come against a Spain team that controlled the entire World Cup final. And, after a tournament riddled with controversy, the side didn't even show a glimpse of greatness in the match. Instead, their highlight reel will feature Enzo Fernández's second yellow card in the second half, which equaled a red card. He was ejected and Argentina was forced to continue with 10 players. They finished the fixture with absolutely ZERO shots on goal, far from a performance that they will want to remember.

President Donald Trump was in attendance at the World Cup final along with members of his family and other dignitaries. He has attempted to position himself as an influential decision-maker in the tournament, including taking credit for having Folarin Balogun's red card rescinded. The president was booed when he came on the pitch to award the medals and trophy to Spain and then made a fool of himself by sticking around too long.

We will always love "Yellow," but the Coldplay frontman let us down with the extremely chaotic World Cup halftime show, which he was supposedly in charge of curating. FIFA was clearly gunning for the Super Bowl with the performance, the first of its kind at the global tournament. But where the NFL is thoughtful in getting a single, or sometimes a few, popular stars to deliver an artful performance, World Cup organizers provided a disjointed, throbbing mish mash that was simply hard to watch.

title: John Strong mistakes Matt Damon for Brad Pitt on Fox World Cup final broadcast
description: Fox play-by-play announcer John Strong had a celebrity mix-up during Sunday afternoon’s broadcast of the 2026 FIFA World Cup final between Spain and Argentina at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey. In the first half, the Fox broadcast showed actor Matt Damon in attendance. But Strong thought it was another high-profile actor, Brad Pitt.…
fullText:
Fox play-by-play announcer John Strong had a celebrity mix-up during Sunday afternoon’s broadcast of the 2026 FIFA World Cup final between Spain and Argentina at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey.

In the first half, the Fox broadcast showed actor Matt Damon in attendance. But Strong thought it was another high-profile actor, Brad Pitt.

“Brad Pitt also among the fans here,” Strong said on the Fox broadcast, alongside color commentator Stu Holden.

“Oh, it’s Matt Damon,” Strong realized. “That’s Matt Damon.”

Strong at least realized the gaffe quickly and apologized.

“Apologize to both,” Strong said.

The timing of this mix-up makes it even funnier, with Damon starring as Odysseus in Christopher Nolan’s The Odyssey (which hit theaters on Friday), after Pitt starred in the blockbuster film Troy in 2004.

Damon was at the World Cup final with his wife, Luciana Barroso, who is from Argentina.

Odysseus pulled up to the World Cup final

Matt Damon’s wife, Luciana, is from Argentina

— Yahoo Sports (@YahooSports) July 19, 2026

Unfortunately for them, the Lionel Messi-led Argentina squad wasn’t able to repeat as World Cup champions and lost 1-0 to Spain.

The post John Strong mistakes Matt Damon for Brad Pitt on Fox World Cup final broadcast appeared first on Awful Announcing.

title: Why Jaylen Brown Is Already Coming Up in 76ers Trade Rumors
description: Somehow Jaylen Brown is finding himself in trade conversations once again for no logical reason.
fullText:
Jaylen Brown has not played a game for the Philadelphia 76ers.

Yet, his name is already coming up in trade conversations.

One anonymous member of an NBA front office believes Brown could face the same problem that he did with the Boston Celtics.

"Jaylen is a great player and he is not going to be a locker room problem or anything like that, but if he wasn’t real happy sharing the ball with Jayson Tatum, how happy is he going to be in this set-up? Isn’t it the same issue?" an NBA executive said, per Heavy.

Heavy's Sean Deveney went on to write, "Brown is not a free agent until 2029, but he will be extension eligible next year, and if the Sixers balk on paying him, they could be putting Brown back on the market."

This is nothing more than foolish speculation. Any 76ers fan made nervous by the headline should ease their worries.

As previously mentioned, Brown has not even suited up for Philadelphia yet, meaning there is nothing to base this claim off of other than past chatter. Brown and Tatum won a championship together as a 1A, 1B duo, so clearly playing alongside him was not that much of an issue.

The other illogical piece of this claim is why is there an assumption the Sixers won't extend Brown? He is an All-NBA wing, proven winner, two-way player, and much more, all at only 29 years old.

He may not lineup perfectly with the championship window of the backcourt duo of Tyrese Maxey and V.J. Edgecombe, but he can still help Philadelphia compete for years to come. There's also an argument to be made that Brown's arrival means having a healthy Joel Embiid is a luxury and not a necessity.

Could this be a more realistic conversation to have next offseason? Sure, but to make this claim before his first game as a member of the 76ers is bold.

You'll have the opportunity to engage with other 76ers fans who live and breathe the team, share your thoughts on the organization, and more. If you're asked to sign up or download the Roundtable app... that's free too.

Anthony Pasciolla is the publisher of 76ersRoundtable. He can be reached via email at ampasciolla@gmail.com or followed on X @AnthonyPasci.

title: DeWanna Bonner scores season-high 21, Mercury beat Sun 72-63 to snap 5-game skid
description: DeWanna Bonner scored a season-high 21 points, Kahleah Copper added 12 of her 18 points in the second half, and the Phoenix Mercury beat the Connecticut Sun 72-63 on Sunday to snap a five-game losing streak.  Monique Akoa Makani added 11 points for the Mercury (9-18), who ended a three-game home skid.  Charlisse Leger-Walker converted a three-point play that pulled the Sun within a point with 4:42 left in the game, but Copper answered with a driving layup and then hit two free throws to make it 67-62 less than a minute later.
fullText:
PHOENIX (AP) — DeWanna Bonner scored a season-high 21 points, Kahleah Copper added 12 of her 18 points in the second half, and the Phoenix Mercury beat the Connecticut Sun 72-63 on Sunday to snap a five-game losing streak.

Monique Akoa Makani added 11 points for the Mercury (9-18), who ended a three-game home skid. Natasha Mack (foot) returned from an eight-game absence and had four points, five rebounds, a steal and a block in 20 minutes off the bench.

Charlisse Leger-Walker converted a three-point play that pulled the Sun within a point with 4:42 left in the game, but Copper answered with a driving layup and then hit two free throws to make it 67-62 less than a minute later. Thomas threw an alley-oop to Bonner for a layup and the and-one free throw made it an eight-point lead with 1:44 left.

The Sun (7-19) had their two-game win streak halted. Brittney Griner scored 19 points and Olivia Nelson-Ododa had 12 points and 12 rebounds. Leila Lacan, who scored a career-high 26 in Connecticut's 96-83 win over the Mercury on Friday, had seven points on 2-of-9 shooting and a career-high nine turnovers.

The Mercury scored 22 points off the Sun's season-high 21 turnovers.

Phoenix shot 50% from the field and had 12 assists on 17 made field goals in the first half. The Mercury had a 10-point lead at the break.

Connecticut's Aaliyah Edwards left the game due to a knee injury in the first quarter and did not return.

Aneesah Morrow (reconditioning) and Saniya Rivers (ankle/foot) did not play for the Sun.

Sun: Play Wednesday at Indiana.

Mercury: Visit Los Angeles on Wednesday.

AP WNBA: https://apnews.com/hub/wnba-basketball

title: What Bryson DeChambeau told reporters after his final round of The Open as he skips media again
description: Bryson DeChambeau continued his media blackout after his final round of The Open. Once again, DeChambeau opted against answering questions from reporters at Royal Birkdale, where he finished in a tie for 14th.
fullText:
Bryson DeChambeau continued his media blackout after his final round of The Open.

Once again, DeChambeau opted against answering questions from reporters at Royal Birkdale, where he finished in a tie for 14th.

It represents his best finish across the four major championships this season, with the American having missed the cut at the other three tournaments.

DeChambeau was embroiled in controversy at The Open, receiving a two-shot penalty on Friday, but he will be proud of his overall efforts at Royal Birkdale.

However, his negative side emerged once more late on Sunday after DeChambeau failed to speak to reporters at the Southport course.

Ali Stafford of Sky Sports was present at the venue, and shared the information: “Bryson DeChambeau may have avoided a Grand Slam of missed cuts this week, but he ends having not engaged with the media after an eighth consecutive major round.

“He walked straight past the mixed zone after a final-round 72, saying ‘hope you guys have a great night’ as he walked past the waiting media.

“He then took a few smiling selfies with fans before heading away. A week of highs and lows for the two-time major champion.”

It is nothing new from DeChambeau, who is clearly very unhappy with the way he has been treated by the media in recent times.

DeChambeau may not have enjoyed the perfect week at The Open, but his status in the game was perfectly summarised at Royal Birkdale.

He just played out one of the biggest roller-coasters in recent major history, with his two-shot penalty for inadvertently improving his intended backswing taking centre stage.

The American showed great strength to bounce back from that setback, however only after he really let himself down by threatening to not play on Saturday because of his punishment.

Moreover, as he always does, the LIV Golf superstar divided opinion, with Rory McIlroy saying DeChambeau deserved his penalty, while many of his compatriots jumped to his defence.

And at the very end of it all, he let down the media once again, but made sure that the fans were not left in that same position.

title: Former Spartans guard makes NBA Summer League debut this weekend
description: See how former Spartans guard Tre Holloman fared in this NBA Summer League debut on Saturday
fullText:
A former Spartans point guard made his NBA Summer League debut this past weekend in a limited role off the bench.

Former Michigan State point guard Tre Holloman played in his first NBA Summer League game on Saturday, scoring two points and two assists in five minutes of action with the Orlando Magic. He ended the game with a plus/minus of four points as well.

Holloman played his first three years of his collegiate career with the Spartans before transferring to NC State for this past season. He appeared in 106 games during his three years with the Spartans, averaging 9.1 points and 3.7 assists per game during his final season in 2024-25.

In his lone year with NC State, Holloman appeared in 32 games (27 of which were starts). He ended the season averaging 9.2 points and 2.1 assists per game with the Wolfpack.

Tre Holloman got to see his first Summer league action in the Orlando Magic’s final game before playoffs.

title: Put on a show, Ty Simpson
description: If all goes according to plan, we won’t see much of Rams QB Ty Simpson after training camp
fullText:
Training camp kicks off this week for the Los Angeles Rams.

Matthew Stafford returns from his 2025 MVP campaign. High-profile additions like Trent McDuffie, Myles Garrett, and Jaylen Watson will make their debuts. Aaron Donald could un-retire soon.

There’s plenty of exciting news surrounding the Rams. No story may be bigger over training camp and the preseason than rookie quarterback and 2026 first-round pick Ty Simpson.

LA’s selection of Simpson sent shockwaves across the NFL world. Instead of drafting another playmaker to get the most out of Stafford’s remaining tenure, the Rams instead opted to prepare for life after their franchise quarterback.

Simpson is going to dominate headlines. The vibe will shift on every positive or negative report from practice. We may even get to see him in the preseason, and it’s all but certain that people will drop sweeping conclusions from a very limited sample size.

Rams fans haven’t seen a show like this since Jared Goff was picked first overall pack in 2016. They aren’t used to young quarterback news cycles and the drama that surrounds them.

It is imperative to stay even keeled. Simpson is the future of the team. It will take time for him to develop. This has always been the Rams’ plan and they have the rare luxury of patience.

So let’s get out in front of the wave and set realistic expectations.

Training camp is not a relevant measuring stick for the likes of Garrett, McDuffie, Watson, or Donald. Their real test will come in Week 1 when the Rams travel to Australia to take on the San Francisco 49ers.

But camp is effectively Simpson’s Super Bowl. He may not even play in the preseason.

So before the rookie gets put on mothballs, why not put on a show and demonstrate why the Rams felt confident selecting him at thirteenth overall?

If all goes according to plan, training camp and preseason are our only opportunities to check in on Simpson’s development. Then time will freeze and we will hold all prior conclusions until this time next year, of which Simpson should either enter as the presumed starter or in store for another year as Stafford’s understudy.

Simpson’s debut can be spectacularly awful or promising. It doesn’t matter or have any impact on the outlook of his NFL career. He just needs to let loose and show a few glimpses of what the Rams saw ahead of the draft.

I view the backup job behind Stafford as entirely Stetson Bennett’s to lose.

Third quarterbacks are effectively inactive on gamedays unless the top two are injured. That means if something happens to the starter, the backup is going to play. There is no alternative realistically.

That is exactly the reason why Simpson should not enter the regular season as Stafford’s backup. He shouldn’t play until Sean McVay believes he is ready and applies his metaphorical stamp of approval.

Could Simpson be ready before Week 1? I guess it’s possible. I’m more inclined to believe that Simpson will need time and the transition could happen at the earliest somewhere around the season’s midpoint.

So in short, don’t panic when Simpson loses the quarterback battle. Bennett could easily win the job but not truly be the better of the two players. The Rams have a difficult decision to make in terms of balancing the short-term and long-term interests of the team.

Simpson may give the Rams a better chance to win this season. Playing him could skewer the remainder of his career and skew perceptions, similar to what happened to Goff as a rookie.

McVay and the Rams do not value the preseason. Any player who holds a meaningful role generally sits out.

LA should also try to avoid a disastrous JJ McCarthy-type injury with Simpson, as that would significantly hinder his development.

But if the backup quarterback battle is truly “rigged”, meaning it’s Bennett’s to lose, then Bennett shouldn’t play in the preseason either. By pure process of elimination, Simpson should play.

The real question is if Simpson plays, how much is there to really learn?

Teams run vanilla offenses in exhibition games in order to preserve secrecy ahead of the regular season. Simpson likely wouldn’t even be running McVay’s real plays. Defenses also keep schemes, coverages, and blitzes vanilla too. It’s football but not necessarily real football.

And if Simpson does play, people will attempt to make up their mind about the trajectory of the quarterback’s career even though (1) we knew he needed time to develop behind the scenes and the Rams are uniquely equipped to afford that opportunity, (2) he’s not running the Rams’ best plays, (3) isn’t playing with a normal supporting cast, and (4) doesn’t have much playing experience under his belt.

Patience has always been key for Ty Simpson. Even though we are set to get our first glimpses of his NFL career, fans should also be open minded and patient. Training camp struggles do not indicate that the Rams wasted the thirteenth overall pick or that Simpson will never be a good NFL quarterback. Positive signs should also be taken with a grain of salt.

We will overreact because this is our only opportunity to see Simpson until this time next year. We shouldn’t. Just enjoy the show.

title: ‘You Don’t Have to Pout All Week’ — Joe Burrow Reveals Justin Jefferson’s Frustration After Rough Patch at LSU
description: Since the two were drafted in the 2020 NFL Draft, both Joe Burrow and Justin Jefferson have solidified themselves as some of the best the position has to offer. While one has already gone to the Super Bowl, the other is cementing himself as one of the greatest wide receivers in history.
fullText:
Since the two were drafted in the 2020 NFL Draft, both Joe Burrow and Justin Jefferson have solidified themselves as some of the best the position has to offer. While one has already gone to the Super Bowl, the other is cementing himself as one of the greatest wide receivers in history.

However, before they were running roughshod over the league, the two formed an unstoppable duo at the collegiate level for the LSU Tigers. In a surprising anecdote, though, the superstar quarterback revealed that the two got into an occasional tiff.

Today, Burrow is a three-time Pro Bowler and has led his team to an AFC Championship Game victory in his short career. On the other hand, Jefferson already has four All-Pro nods, alongside an Offensive Player of the Year award in his collection.

But before their individual success in the NFL, the two were wreaking havoc for LSU. As Burrow walked away with the Heisman Trophy, he set the NCAA single-season record for touchdown passes and led the nation in passing yards.

That same year, Jefferson led the NCAA in receptions and had 1,540 yards and 18 touchdowns to his name. The two powered a legendary offense en route to a College Football Playoff National Championship.

But even with all of their highs together, it wasn’t always smooth sailing between them. Making an appearance at the Fanatics Fest 2026, which has taken over New York for a four-day extravaganza, Burrow made a unique confession.

BUILD YOUR OWN TOP 100: PFN’s FREE NFL Top 100 Builder

“I think Justin went two games without a touchdown, and he didn’t talk to me for the entire next week. I was like, ‘I’ll get you a touchdown, bro. You don’t have to pout all week on me.'”

After that brutal stretch, Burrow kept his word, and Jefferson responded with a five-week stretch where he had nine touchdowns, including three games over 100 yards, one of which was a season-high 227-yard showing in the College Football Playoff semifinal against Oklahoma.

BE THE GM OF YOUR FAVORITE TEAM: PFN’s FREE NFL Mock Draft Simulator

Since coming to the league, Jefferson hadn’t suffered any such problems, eclipsing 1,000 receiving yards every season. Last year, though, was a major blip in that regard. As J.J. McCarthy stood as far from a finished product, the four-time Pro Bowler put up career-lows with 1,048 yards and just two touchdowns.

To put it in perspective, his previous lows in both categories were 1,074 receiving yards and five touchdowns, which came during a year in which he only played 10 games in total, as opposed to the full 17-game quota in 2025.

As a result, rumors of a potential trade continue to circulate. While he might not be directly saying anything, it seems like Jefferson is back to a similar style that he followed with Burrow during that stretch.

title: Spain win World Cup after Messi's Argentina fall flat
description: Spain won their second World Cup on Sunday, collecting the trophy from US President Donald Trump after Ferran Torres' extra-time strike broke the stubborn resistance of Lionel Messi's Argentina.Argentina coach Lionel Scaloni admitted Spain had deserved to win.
fullText:
Spain won their second World Cup on Sunday, collecting the trophy from US President Donald Trump after Ferran Torres' extra-time strike broke the stubborn resistance of Lionel Messi's Argentina.

More than 80,000 people crammed into the MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey, to witness a tense, tight final punctuated by cynical Argentine fouls that ended 1-0.

The defending champions struggled to compete with dominant Spain and were left with a mountain to climb when Enzo Fernandez was sent off in the 93rd minute for a second yellow card after a reckless challenge on defender Pau Cubarsi.

Argentina barely had an attempt on goal in the entire match but held on grimly, with goalkeeper Emiliano Martinez making a series of saves.

The 2010 winners, who had peppered their opponents' goal, finally broke the deadlock early in the second half of extra-time.

The goal came from a brilliant knock-back by Nico Williams for fellow substitute Torres, who smashed the ball high into the net in the 106th minute.

At the end, the Spain players cavorted on the pitch while Messi was in tears as he trudged up to collect his runners-up medal.

The ceremony was presided over by Trump, who was loudly booed as he walked onto the pitch at the end of the game with Gianni Infantino, the chief of world football's governing body FIFA.

Trump had to be asked by Infantino to move to the side as he lingered on stage while the Spanish team held the trophy aloft.

The 80-year-old even did a quick version of his trademark "Trump dance".

An emotional Spain coach Luis de la Fuente said his players were a "source of pride" for the nation.

Spain captain Rodri, who won the player of the tournament award, said it was hard to put his feelings into words, 14 months after he returned from a serious knee injury.

"I think now we're like in a cloud," he said. "It's been a very tough time. I just want the new generations to see my example as an opportunity -- when you go down you can rise again."

Messi, 39, may have played his last World Cup game and he can take enormous credit for dragging his ageing team to the final after they were pushed to the limit in knockout games against Egypt and England.

But on Sunday, he was largely shut out of the game as Spain imposed their smooth passing game and refused to shirk physical challenges.

The South Americans had been aiming to become the first team since Brazil in 1962 to win consecutive World Cups. But Spain were faster, slicker and younger.

Argentina coach Lionel Scaloni admitted Spain had deserved to win.

"They were the better side -- that's the truth -- but I'll cherish the memory of (this team), of what they achieved, and of the value of making it this far," said Scaloni, who later broke down in tears at his post-game press conference.

"We have to place immense value on this because it takes so much effort," he added.

Musical headliners Madonna, Shakira, Justin Bieber, BTS and Coldplay were joined by Brazilian soccer icons Ronaldinho and Ronaldo and "Ted Lasso" actors in the first-ever World Cup final half-time show.

The star-packed, Super Bowl-style extravaganza also featured a tribute to late football icon Pele, and a rendition of "Seven Nation Army" led by Gustavo Dudamel and featuring musicians from the US, Venezuela and Iran.

On Saturday, England beat France 6-4 in a high-scoring thriller to seal third place -- their best finish since they won the tournament in 1966.

Kylian Mbappe became the competition's all-time leading scorer with 22 goals.

The France forward also won the Golden Boot with 10 goals, two ahead of Messi, finishing as top scorer for the second World Cup in a row.

The build-up to the World Cup in Canada, Mexico and the United States was dominated by fears over sky-high prices, geopolitical tensions and concerns that brutal summer heat would make matches unplayable.

But the biggest, most complex edition of football's showpiece ever staged -- expanded from 32 teams to 48 -- will be remembered for thrilling football and a feel-good factor.

Trump has hailed the tournament as "the most successful sporting event, maybe in the history of the world".

title: Where did MSU's Pat Fitzgerald land in CBS Sports' annual hot seat rankings?
description: See where Michigan State's Pat Fitzgerald landed in CBS Sports' hot seat rankings for the upcoming season
fullText:
Michigan State head coach Pat Fitzgerald is considered one of the safest coaches in all of college football as we near the 2026 season.

CBS Sports has released their annual hot seat ratings and rankings for all of the FBS head coaches, and to no surprise, the newly hired Fitzgerald is sitting on a very cool seat at the moment. On a rating system of zero through five, Fitzgerald landed with a 0.7 rating -- which is considered "untouchable" at the moment.

Fitzgerald is set to enter his first season as the Spartans head coach after taking over for Jonathan Smith, who was fired after only two years at Michigan State. The man who hired Fitzgerald, though, is no longer around as former athletic director J Batt is now off to Kentucky after only one year of running the Spartans' athletics department. So it's interesting to see Fitzgerald have such a low hot seat rating among his peers.

For some perspective, new Michigan head coach Kyle Whittingham has a rating of 1.5 -- which is notably higher than Fitzgerald. One would assume that would be higher due to the Wolverines having higher expectations to win big this year compared to the Spartans.

title: Zlatan Ibrahimovic announces Fox decision after World Cup
description: Excitement arose among soccer fans around the world when it was announced that Zlatan Ibrahimovic will serve as an analyst for the FIFA World Cup. He worked alongside Alexi Lalas and Thierry Henry for Fox Sports’ coverage and gave entertaining…
fullText:
Excitement arose among soccer fans around the world when it was announced that Zlatan Ibrahimovic will serve as an analyst for the FIFA World Cup. He worked alongside Alexi Lalas and Thierry Henry for Fox Sports’ coverage and gave entertaining insights about games and players.

However, the legend announced after Sunday’s final that he will not return. He bid the network goodbye and stressed that he will not cover future World Cups.

MORE: Argentina World Cup run ends with wild brawl after whistle vs Spain

“”And this is also my farewell to the studio,” Ibrahimovic said. “This was the first and last time for me. So, take care, everybody.”

Ibrahimovic anchored the Los Angeles-based studio team, contributing to pregame, halftime and postgame shows. He gave highly entertaining analysis in true Zlatan fashion, endearing him more to fans who supported him throughout his playing career.

The 44-year-old hung his cleats up for good in 2022 following AC Milan’s final game of the season. He started his senior club career with Malmo FF before playing for Ajax, Juventus and Inter Milan. Ibrahimovic then moved to Barcelona, AC Milan, Paris Saint-German and Manchester United. He also suited up for the LA Galaxy before returning to Italy to end his career there.

Ibrahimovic won league titles in the Netherlands, Italy, Spain, France and England. He also suited up for Sweden in international competitions, establishing himself as one of the best forwards in the world. His flamboyant playing style and larger-than-life persona made him an influential figure in the sport.

title: NBA Reddit thinks Cooper Flagg grew 2 inches
description: This would be big news for the star out of Duke.
fullText:
NBA Reddit thinks Cooper Flagg grew 2 inches originally appeared on The Sporting News. Add The Sporting News as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

Cooper Flagg is already a rising NBA superstar.

If the Dallas Mavericks' phenom out of Duke grew a couple inches? He'd be even better.

That's the theory that's been going around the Internet for a while this summer.

Some folks on X were posting about it. There's a discussion thread on Reddit about it, too.

Internet sleuths noticed that Flagg liked a post on Instagram that said he grew two inches, from 6-foot-9 to 6-foot-11.

There have been no public comments on the matter, though, and no official listings anywhere that show Flagg at that taller height.

So at this point, it's all a bit of speculation.

MORE: Caleb Wilson fires a subtle shot at AJ Dybantsa, Darryn Peterson

Presumably at some point, either Flagg will be asked about it or the Mavs will release an official roster with a height listing.

In the meantime, commenters on that Reddit post are speculating that Flagg looks taller next to his teammate Dereck Lively II than he did last year.

It wouldn't be crazy if Flagg was still growing. He's just 19 and won't turn 20 years old until late December.

Most players don't change their height listing much, if at all, after entering the NBA. But the Spurs' Victor Wembanyama did, and it's certainly not unheard of.

Some players actually grow more and don't change their height. Kevin Garnett was almost certainly taller than he was listed at, for example.

The reality is that if Flagg stars on the court, it doesn't really matter what his basketball card says he is.

But if he's actually taller, opponents should look out. That makes Flagg that much scarier to deal with.
